[dialogue] Nothing... Your father, it’s just 
[dialogue] too much to deal with right now.

[character] WILL
[dialogue] I’ll mow lawns. Paint fences. 
[dialogue] Whatever.

[character] SHARON
[dialogue] It’s not that.

[action] She loses it for a moment, then composes herself.

[character] SHARON (CONT’D)
[dialogue] Your brother just looked forward to 
[dialogue] this trip so much. That park was 
[dialogue] his favorite place in the world.

[action] She takes Will’s carton and retreats into the kitchen. Will 
[action] is left all alone, TV blaring, spoon hanging over nothing.
[page_number] 38.

[character] WILL
[dialogue] Mine too.

[scene_heading] INT. BEVERLY’S APARTMENT - NIGHT

[action] A slummy apartment over Lower Main Street. A Red Sox game 
[action] blares on from somewhere down the hall.  We track down the 
[action] hallway and PAN just as Beverly starts to unzip her pants and 
[action] close the door.

[scene_heading] INT. BEVERLY’S APARTMENT, BATHROOM - NIGHT

[action] Beverly finishes peeing.  She reaches for toilet paper but 
[action] there isn’t any.  She rifles through the toiletries under the 
[action] old fashioned sink basin looking for anything she can use- 

[centered] CHILD’S VOICE (O.S.)
[] Help me.

[action] Beverly looks around for the voice, startled.

[] CHILD’S VOICE (O.S.) (CONT’D)
[] Help me, Beverly.

[action] It comes from THE SINK DRAIN, above her head. 

[action] She stands, her pants still around her ankles, leaning 
[action] forward over the basin, looking down into the dark void.

[character] BEVERLY
[dialogue] Is s-someone there?

[action] Nothing. BEV’S EYE PEERS DOWN THROUGH THE CIRCLE OF LIGHT.

[centered] CHILD’S VOICE (O.S.)
[] We all want to meet you, Beverly.

[action] She gasps, backpedaling, pulling up her pants.

[character] BEVERLY
[dialogue] Who are you?

[action] The single voice turns into a cacophony, bubbling up through 
[action] the ages.

[centered] CHILD’S VOICE (O.S.)
[] I’m Matthew... I’m Dorsey... I’m 
[] Veronica... I’m Georgie... Come 
[] play with us down here... Come play 
[] with the clown... You’ll float, 
[] Beverly. Oh how you’ll float...
[page_number] 39.

[action] Terrified, Beverly dashes out.

[scene_heading] INT. BEVERLY’S APARTMENT, LIVING ROOM - NIGHT

[action] Bev finds HER FATHER (40s) asleep in front of the TV, still 
[action] in his janitor’s uniform from Derry Home Hospital. His 
[action] toolbelt is thrown up on the coffee table. 

[action] She tip-toes over and steals the TAPE MEASURER.

[scene_heading] INT. BEVERLY’S APARTMENT, BATHROOM - NIGHT

[action] Beverly stands over the sink basin, tape measurer in hand. 
[action] The voice is silent. 

[action] She unfurls the tape into the drain, its tip disappearing 
[action] into the void.

[action] FOOT BY FOOT she snakes the tape into the drain, until it’s 
[action] fully extended at 20 feet. She waits for a voice. Nothing.

[action] Slowly, she begins to reel the tape back in, counting down 
[action] the length as it winds back in. 16 feet... 15 feet... 14 
[action] feet... AT 13 FEET VISCOUS BLOOD COATS THE TAPE.

[action] BEVERLY GASPS and drops the measurer. It goes clattering into 
[action] the sink, the tape coiling up like a snake, blood flickering 
[action] everywhere as she stumbles back, tripping into the shower.

[action] BLOOD GOUTS UP FROM THE SINK DRAIN 

[action] Like a demonic ejaculation -- blood splatters the mirror, the 
[action] wallpaper, bouncing off walls and covering Beverly. She 
[action] SCREAMS and runs out the door...

[scene_heading] INT. BEVERLY’S APARTMENT, LIVING ROOM - NIGHT

[action] ...into her father, who comes charging up the hall. She 
[action] screams again, recoiling away.

[character] MR. MARSH
[dialogue] What the devil’s gotten into you, 
[dialogue] girl?

[character] BEVERLY
[dialogue] The bathroom! Daddy, in the 
[dialogue] bathroom--

[action] His arm shoots out and GRABS HER WRIST LIKE A VICE.
[page_number] 40.

[character] MR. MARSH
[dialogue] Someone peeking at you, Bevvie? 
[dialogue] Huh?

[character] BEVERLY
[dialogue] No... the sink... in the sink...

[action] He stalks past, dragging her behind him. They step into...

[scene_heading] INT. BEVERLY’S APARTMENT, BATHROOM - NIGHT

[action] Mr. Marsh looks around, eyes wide, blood splattered 
[action] everywhere, but the blood doesn’t register with him. 

[action] He shoves her against the wall. Hard.

[character] MR. MARSH
[dialogue] You know I hate gettin’ rough with 
[dialogue] you. I never do when you don’t 
[dialogue] deserve it.

[action] He points back to the gory sink.

[character] MR. MARSH (CONT’D)
[dialogue] What’s my tape measurer doin’ outta 
[dialogue] my toolbelt?

[action] He steps over and grabs the BLOOD-COATED TAPE, clipping it 
[action] onto his belt, blood on his hands now too.  He doesn’t seem 
[action] to see any of it. Only Beverly can. She realizes this.

[character] MR. MARSH (CONT’D)
[dialogue] Answer me girl.

[action] He raises his hand.  

[character] BEVERLY
[dialogue] The sink... I was... I thought I 
[dialogue] saw a spider...

[action] Uncertain whether he’ll hit her anyway, she closes her eyes 
[action] and prepares for a smacking.

[character] MR. MARSH
[parenthetical] (smiling)
[dialogue] A spider? 

[character] BEVERLY
[dialogue] Yes, sir. I’m sorry for waking you.

[action] He lowers his hand, gentle now, proprietary.  
[page_number] 41.

[character] MR. MARSH
[dialogue] I worry about you, Bevvie. I worry 
[dialogue] a lot. 

[action] Smoothing out her hair over her forehead.  The blood on her 
[action] face like finger paint.  This is when he scares her the most.

[character] MR. MARSH (CONT’D)
[dialogue] You know why I don’t drink or smoke 
[dialogue] or chase after women like other 
[dialogue] fathers do?

[character] BEVERLY
[dialogue] Because you love momma?

[character] MR. MARSH
[dialogue] Not just her, Bevvie. You. You’re 
[dialogue] almost a woman now, too. And I got 
[dialogue] me all I need right here at home.

[action] He rests his forehead against hers, breathes in her scent, 
[action] goes back down the hall to his game.

[scene_heading] INT. DETAIL ON CEILING - NIGHT

[action] ANGLE ON a wet, dark spot as it grows on a white plaster 
[action] ceiling, the first droplet of water forming.  SLOW MOTION as 
[action] it breaks away and falls through space...

[transition] SMASH CUT TO:

[scene_heading] INT. WILL’S BEDROOM - NIGHT 

[action] ...onto Will’s head. He doesn’t stir.  But then it is 
[action] followed by SEVERAL OTHERS.  Finally, he awakes.  Looks up.

[action] A leak in the ceiling.  

[action] Will flips back his covers.

[scene_heading] INT. BATHROOM HALLWAY - NIGHT

[action] Will grabs a bucket from a utility closet and, walking back, 
[action] sees Georgie's room’s door open...

[scene_heading] INT. GEORGE’S BEDROOM - NIGHT

[action] Will enters, the room frozen in time. GEORGIE’S TOY BOX is 
[action] open.  Photographs and posters line the walls.  
[page_number] 42.

[action] STAR WARS bedsheets crisp and military tight on the bed.  A 
[action] Lego Turtle on the night stand.  Will picks it up.

[action] Will sits down, Turtle in hand, and indulges in the sadness 
[action] that has engulfed his home for months.  The loss of his 
[action] brother hitting him full force, he closes his eyes to fight 
[action] back the tears, but cannot.  

[action] He cries for George.  Photographs of George, illuminated by 
[action] passing cars, seem to watch him from the walls.

[action] A SHADOW seems to stretch across Georgie’s room from the 
[action] doorway. 

[action] Will looks up but no one is there.

[scene_heading] INT. CORRIDOR - WILL’S HOUSE - NIGHT

[action] Will steps out of George’s room and finds DARK FOOTPRINTS IN 
[action] THE CARPET.  He leans down and touches one -- squishy and 
[action] wet.  They track down the dark staircase. 

[action] Will, heart pounding, follows the wet footprints down the 
[action] stairs, careful not to step in any of them.

[character] WILL
[dialogue] Hello?  Dad?

[scene_heading] INT. DOWNSTAIRS - WILL’S HOUSE - DAY

[action] At the bottom of the stairs Will finds the footprints lead 
[action] TOWARD THE KITCHEN WHERE...

[character] WILL
[dialogue] Ma?

[action] Will sees a YELLOW FLASH of something ducking around the 
[action] corner. Startled, Will drops the Lego turtle, which smashes 
[action] into pieces and scatters across the wood floor... 

[action] ***WILL HEARS GEORGIE’S “CODE” CALL FROM THE OPENING SCENE.  
[action] IT COMES FROM THE KITCHEN***

[action] Will looks back up the stairs half-expecting his parents to 
[action] wake up. Nothing but an eerie silence. He gathers his courage 
[action] and follows the wet footprints into...
[page_number] 43.

[scene_heading] INT. KITCHEN - WILL’S HOUSE - DAY

[action] Will steps in, face to face with THE CELLAR DOOR, the door 
[action] creaking closed, light snapped on behind it, footprints 
[action] disappearing down into the cellar. 

[character] WILL
[parenthetical] (voice quivering)
[dialogue] Georgie?

[action] ***WILL RESPONDS WITH HIS OWN “CODE” CALL. THERE IS NO 
[action] IMMEDIATE RESPONSE***

[action] Will slowly approaches the door and reaches out for the 
[action] handle, but stops with second thoughts. He starts backing 
[action] away, too spooked to go down, when he hears...

[action] A CHILD WEEPING BEYOND THE DOOR, then...

[action] ***GEORGIE’S “CODE” CALL, COMING FROM THE BASEMENT***

[scene_heading] INT. CELLAR - WILL’S HOUSE - DAY

[action] Will opens the door, rickety stairs leading down into 
[action] darkness, the weeper somewhere in the recesses, along with 
[action] the steady sound of a LEAK. 

[action] Steeling himself, Will descends. He gets to the last few 
[action] rungs and is thrown off for a moment seeing HIS REFLECTION in 
[action] the basement floor, as if it were A BLACK MIRROR. He realizes 

[subheading] THE CELLAR HAS FLOODED

[action] Will looks up toward the leak/weeping sound and sees, curled 
[action] in the same corner where we had that opening POV...

[action] GEORGE IN HIS YELLOW SLICKER.

[action] Rain rolling off him like he’s still in a storm flooding the 
[action] cellar. He looks up at Will.

[character] GEORGE
[dialogue] Why’d you make me go, Willy?

[character] WILL
[dialogue] Georgie?

[action] Will moves to step into the water when he hesitates, holding 
[action] his foot over it. He looks to Georgie whose expression of 
[action] distress seems insincere.

[character] GEORGE
[dialogue] Help me, Willy. 
[page_number] 44.

[action] Will grabs a rake from the wall and pokes its tip into the 
[action] water.  It’s a good six inches deep.

[character] GEORGE (CONT’D)
[dialogue] You’ll float.

[action] Water and muck pour from his mouth as he says these last 
[action] words, an endless cascade.

[action] Will recoils. Georgie glides over the water towards Will, and 
[action] we realize there is a creature beyond it, the colors of a 
[action] clown’s costume, PENNYWISE, propping up Georgie’s body like a 
[action] MEAT PUPPET.

[action] Horrified, Will races up the stairs as...

[action] PENNYWISE crosses the flooded basement and scuffles up the 
[action] steps after Will, arms and legs splayed out like a charging 
[action] crocodile, Georgie’s limp corpse strapped to his back. 

[action] Will slams the door on Pennywise’s/Georgie’s gross face.

[scene_heading] INT. WILL DENBROUGH’S HOUSE, WILL’S ROOM

[action] Will slams the door of his room and hides in his bed.  
[action] Panting.  The DOOR KNOB starts to rattle. 

[action] Drip.  Will looks up.  The leak from the ceiling getting 
[action] worse.  The DOOR KNOB rattles even more.  Shaking until it 
[action] seems like its going to fall off.

[action] Silence.  Will approaches the door.  He grabs a baseball bat 
[action] and prepares to attack.  Swinging open the door, ready to 
[action] swing... it’s his FATHER.

[character] ZACH
[dialogue] Will.

[action] Seeing the bat.

[character] ZACH (CONT’D)
[dialogue] Easy!  What’s wrong?  What’s all 
[dialogue] the noise about?

[action] Will exhales, safety. 

[character] WILL
[dialogue] There’s a leak in my ceiling.  Then 
[dialogue] I went to check the cellar and its 
[dialogue] flooded and--

[action] Zack looks at Will’s ceiling.
[page_number] 45.

[character] ZACH
[dialogue] Where’s the leak, son?

[action] Will points at it but nothing registers for Zach.

[scene_heading] INT. WILL’S HOUSE, BASEMENT DOOR - MOMENTS LATER

[action] Will and Zach stand over the stairwell.  Zach turns the light 
[action] on and walks down, much to Will’s horror, into a puddle of 
[action] water.

[character] ZACH
[dialogue] Dry as a bone, Will.  Sure it 
[dialogue] wasn’t just a dream?

[action] Will holds himself, too freaked out to speak.

[scene_heading] EXT. EDGE OF THE BARRENS - MORNING

[action] Will, Stan, Richie, Ben and Eddie stand at the edge of a 
[action] police cordon.  Beyond, CHIEF BORTON and A LINE OF POLICE 
[action] OFFICERS, SHERIFF’S DEPUTIES and VOLUNTEERS sweep the woods 
[action] with blood hounds. 

[character] RICHIE
[dialogue] But we got a project we’re working 
[dialogue] on out there.

[character] CHIEF BORTON
[dialogue] Barren’s will be off limits for 
[dialogue] now, kids.  Go on into town and 
[dialogue] play there.

[action] They all look on in disappointment.  Then pick up their bikes 
[action] and head back into town.

[action] A dog picks up a scent near A MORLOCK HOLE -- a cement 
[action] cylinder that sticks about four feet out of the ground with a 
[action] vented iron manhole cover stamped:  DERRY SEWER DEPARTMENT . A 
[action] drone comes from somewhere deep within.

[action] Officer Butch Bowers arrives with news for Chief Borton.

[character] OFFICER BOWERS
[dialogue] We’ve got another one, Chief.

[character] CHIEF BORTON
[dialogue] Another what?

[character] OFFICER BOWERS
[dialogue] Kid gone missing.
[page_number] 46.

[character] CHIEF BORTON
[dialogue] Dear Lord.  Who is it?

[character] OFFICER BOWERS
[dialogue] One of my son’s friends.  Patrick 
[dialogue] Hockstettler.  

[action] PAN OVER to reveal Travis, waiting meekly inside Officer 
[action] Bowers’ squad car.

[character] CHIEF BORTON
[dialogue] The family notified you directly?

[action] Officer Bowers nods for Travis to approach.

[character] OFFICER BOWERS
[dialogue] Then I did a little investigating 
[dialogue] of my own.
[parenthetical] (to Travis)
[dialogue] Tell him what you told me, boy.

[action] Travis doesn’t know where to start.

[character] OFFICER BOWERS (CONT’D)
[dialogue] Tell him, damn it!  Tell him who 
[dialogue] you last saw him with. 
[dialogue] (he turns to Chief Borton)
[dialogue] The negro boy from outside of town.  
[dialogue] The ones who run the abatoir.

[scene_heading] INT. BEVERLY’S APARTMENT - MORNING

[action] BEN’S HAIKU is propped on a night stand. 

[action] Beverly stirs awake.  Her sheets and pillows are smeared with 
[action] blood.  Her face crusted with dried gore.  

[action] Groggy, and nearly sleep walking, she sits up and walks out 
[action] without taking stock.

[scene_heading] INT. BEVERLY’S BATHROOM - MORNING

[action] Bev opens the door, wiping her eyes she approximates the 
[action] sink, bends over to wash her face and eyes.  With the first 
[action] crust of blood removed from her eyelids, she looks into the 
[action] mirror and let’s out a choked, exhaling GASP of horror.

[action] Surrounded by brown and crusty wallpaper, is Beverly’s 
[action] realization that the blood was not a dream. 

[action] Beverly’s Mom appears in the doorway.  Beverly turns to her, 
[action] speechless, in shock.
[page_number] 47.

[character] MRS. MARSH 
[dialogue] Darling, sometimes I look at myself 
[dialogue] in the morning and think the same 
[dialogue] thing.  

[action] Beverly tiptoes out of the horror scene.  She shyly looks at 
[action] her mother as she dries her hair over a blood-drenched sink, 
[action] oblivious to it all. 

[character] MRS. MARSH (CONT’D)
[dialogue] Look at these roots.

[action] Beverly is speechless.

[scene_heading] INT. HANDLON ABATTOIR - DAY

[action] Puddles of BLOOD seem to lap like small waves towards us.  
[action] PAN UP to reveal Mike and Leroy sweeping the blood and water 
[action] out of the slaughtering pen.

[action] Officer Bowers followed by SEVERAL POLICE OFFICERS enter the 
[action] facility.

[character] OFFICER BOWERS
[dialogue] Leroy Hanlon?

[action] Leroy turns and notices the officers for the first time.

[character] OFFICER BOWERS (CONT’D)
[dialogue] We need to bring in your boy for 
[dialogue] questioning.

[character] LEROY
[dialogue] For what?

[character] OFFICER BOWERS
[dialogue] Police business.

[action] Mike turns to his father, frightened, shaking his head.

[character] LEROY
[dialogue] We need a lawyer?

[action] He looks at Mike, searching for any indication what this 
[action] could be about.  Travis appears in the distance and Mike 
[action] instantly knows.

[character] MIKE
[dialogue] Dad, it’s-  

[action] Officer Bowers roughly grabs Mike by the neck and starts to 
[action] lead him out.
[page_number] 48.

[character] OFFICER BOWERS
[dialogue] Let’s go, boy.

[character] LEROY
[dialogue] Hey!  No need for that.

[action] He grabs Officer Bowers by the arm.  Bowers quickly and 
[action] excessively puts Leroy in a wrist lock and throttles him 
[action] against the killing pen fence. Leroy moans in pain.

[character] OFFICER BOWERS
[dialogue] Search this place, now.  It’s the 
[dialogue] perfect place to hide a body if you 
[dialogue] ever needed to.

[action] Officer Bowers looks around in disgust.  Travis smiles from 
[action] the safety of distance and local power.

[character] LEROY
[dialogue] This ain’t right!  You need a 
[dialogue] warrant, you can’t just-

[action] Leroy seizes his arm, then his chest, then crumples to the 
[action] ground, losing consciousness in the puddle of blood. 

[character] MIKE
[dialogue] Dad!

[action] Mike tries to rush for him but he is forcefully escorted to a 
[action] waiting police car.  Other Officers look to Bowers for 
[action] direction.  Officer Bowers turns Leroy over.

[character] OFFICER BOWERS
[dialogue] Call an ambulance.  

[scene_heading] EXT. BEN’S HOUSE, BEDROOM - DAY

[action] ALL of the boys have gathered in Ben’s room.  An aimless 
[action] summer morning with nothing to do.  They play with his junk 
[action] and are generally turning his room over.  Will examines a 
[action] pair of walkie-talkies not unlike his own.

[character] RICHIE
[dialogue] Whoa, what’s with the history 
[dialogue] project, Benny-boy?

[action] Richie notices that Ben’s walls are covered in xerox copies 
[action] and historical drawings, all relating to Derry’s history.
[page_number] 49.

[character] BEN
[dialogue] Oh, uh, when I first moved here I 
[dialogue] didn’t have anyone to play with or 
[dialogue] anything, so I just started hanging 
[dialogue] out in the Library.

[action] All of the boys turn and look at Ben.  Seriously?

[character (DUAL)] RICHIE
[dialogue (DUAL)] What are you, the “Reading 
[dialogue (DUAL)] Rainbow”?
[character (DUAL)] EDDIE
[dialogue (DUAL)] He’s a regular Jordy Laforge.

[action] Stan takes a closer look.

[character] STAN
[dialogue] Why is it all, like, people getting 
[dialogue] killed and missing kids and stuff?

[character] BEN
[dialogue] I don’t know.  There’s just a lot 
[dialogue] of that here.  Been that way since 
[dialogue] the original settlers.

[action] Will, who was more interested in a Viewmaster, turns and 
[action] looks at the walls now. Sees a copy of an old-timey document 
[action] with 300 signatures. INCORPORATION OF THE TOWNSHIP OF DERRY.

[character] WILL
[dialogue] You have a copy of the Town 
[dialogue] Charter.  Seriously?

[character] BEN
[dialogue] Derry started as a logging camp 
[dialogue] before it was officially on any 
[dialogue] maps.  300 people signed the 
[dialogue] charter that made Derry Derry.  A 
[dialogue] month later they all disappeared, 
[dialogue] without a trace. 

[character] RICHIE
[dialogue] The entire town?

[action] Eddie is freaked out by this revelation.

[character] BEN
[dialogue] The people, the livestock, 
[dialogue] everything that was alive.

[character (DUAL)] STAN
[dialogue (DUAL)] Jesus.
[character (DUAL)] EDDIE
[dialogue (DUAL)] Holy fuck.

[character] RICHIE
[parenthetical] (to Stan)
[dialogue] Aren’t you supposed to be Jewish?
[page_number] 50.

[action] Stan thumps Richie in the stomach while still paying 
[action] attention to Ben.

[character] BEN
[dialogue] People in neighboring settlements 
[dialogue] thought it was Indians or something 
[dialogue] but there was no sign of an attack.  
[dialogue] The only clue was a well house that 
[dialogue] was burnt down to charcoal.

[character] RICHIE
[dialogue] Shit, maybe we could get Derry on 
[dialogue] Unsolved Mysteries!

[action] Ben points out on an old map that has a translucent modern 
[action] map drawn by hand laid over it, his tracing coordinates-

[character] BEN
[dialogue] It was here.  Right on the corner 
[dialogue] of what’s now Witcham and Jackson.

[character] EDDIE
[dialogue] Hey, isn’t that where Will’s 
[dialogue] brother, Georgie-

[action] Richie slaps Eddie in the back of the head.  His fierce eyes 
[action] castigating Eddie for not thinking before speaking, Eddie 
[action] confused, “wha-?”

[character] WILL
[dialogue] Yeah, it’s where Georgie died.

[character (DUAL)] BEN
[dialogue (DUAL)] I’m sorry.  I didn’t-
[character (DUAL)] RICHIE
[dialogue (DUAL)] (to Eddie)
[dialogue (DUAL)] Good job numb nuts.

[character] WILL (CONT’D)
[dialogue] Don’t worry about it.  Why do you 
[dialogue] have all this stuff anyway?  I 
[dialogue] mean.  What are you going to do 
[dialogue] with it?

[action] Will can’t take his eyes off all of the information.

[character] BEN
[dialogue] I don’t know.  It was just a way to 
[dialogue] kill time, I guess.  

[character] RICHIE
[dialogue] It’s summer, dorkus.  If you’re 
[dialogue] gonna kill time, do it right.
[page_number] 51.

[scene_heading] EXT. BASSEY PARK QUARRY, ROPE SWING - LATER

[action] Will, Richie, Eddie, Stan, and Ben, stripped to their tightie-
[action] whities (except Ben who still has a T-shirt), stand in a line 
[action] staring at the edge of a rocky outcropping in a quarry turned 
[action] pond.  The black water of the pond foreboding, endless.  

[character] WILL
[dialogue] Who’s first.

[character] RICHIE
[dialogue] Eddie?

[character] EDDIE
[dialogue] Screw that.

[character] BEN
[dialogue] I’ll go.

[character] EDDIE
[dialogue] Ben, with those cuts you have on 
[dialogue] your chest, I’m not sure if getting 
[dialogue] in this water-

[character] RICHIE
[dialogue] Will you stop with the grey water 
[dialogue] shit.  You make it seem like any 
[dialogue] water we get in is like swimming in 
[dialogue] an out-house.

[character] BEN
[dialogue] What’s grey water?

[character] STAN
[dialogue] Don’t get him started.

[character] WILL
[dialogue] I’ll go first.

[character] BEVERLY (O.C.)
[dialogue] Too late.

[action] The boys turn around to see Bev in a one piece summer dress.  
[action] She pulls off the dress quickly, down to her underwear, 
[action] sprints off the edge and jumps into the water.  BOOM.  
[action] Cannonball.

[action] The boys, not wanting to be showed up by the girl take one 
[action] last look at each other and jump in.  SPLASH! SPLASH! SPLASH! 
[action] SPLASH! SPLASH!

[action] All except for Eddie, who crawls down the rock outcropping 
[action] and stares at the screaming and laughing kids enviously.
[page_number] 52.

[character] RICHIE
[dialogue] What are you waiting for?  Why 
[dialogue] don’t you get in you pussy?

[action] Eddie dips the toes of one of his feet in.  Flabbergasted, 
[action] Richie gives up on motivating Eddie.  

[action] Richie dives under the water and swims beneath the kicking 
[action] legs of all his friends, a shark’s P.O.V.  He doesn’t see...

[action] PENNYWISE floating in from the depths beneath him.

[action] On the surface Ben yelps, something yanking his leg. Richie 
[action] surfaces.

[character] BEN
[dialogue] Very funny, Richie.

[character] RICHIE
[dialogue] I thought so.

[action] Something yanks Richie’s leg too.  Hard.  He and Ben wait for 
[action] whoever it was to surface from the inky water.

[action] Ben sees Will, Bev, and Stan swimming back toward Eddie on 
[action] the edge of the quarry.  They scream and laugh and swim their 
[action] asses back to land.

[scene_heading] EXT. BASSEY PARK QUARRY, SHORE - LATER

[action] The SIX of them now sunbathe on the rocks at the edge of the 
[action] pond.  Their eyes are closed, soaking in the vitamin D, 
[action] except Ben, who can’t take his off of Beverly’s tanning body.

[action] Will sits up and looks out at the water.  It is so black it 
[action] seems other worldly.  Not breaking his stare from the water, 
[action] he addresses the group.

[character] WILL
[dialogue] Do you guys ever feel there’s 
[dialogue] something wrong with Derry.  Like 
[dialogue] it’s, I don’t know, haunted or 
[dialogue] something?

[character] EDDIE 
[dialogue] Haunted how?

[character] WILL
[dialogue] I keep thinking about Ben’s walls.  

[action] Ben, happy to have something else to focus on other than 
[action] Beverly, looks at Will.
[page_number] 53.

[character] BEN
[dialogue] You mean how all sorts of disasters 
[dialogue] and disappearances and stuff keeps 
[dialogue] on happening here?

[character] WILL
[dialogue] Yeah.  It’s strange.

[action] Stan opens his eyes and joins in.

[character] STAN
[dialogue] Like any town, right?  That’s been 
[dialogue] around long enough, bad stuff is 
[dialogue] bound to happen.  I mean, have you 
[dialogue] read the Torah?

[action] Richie, turns over to work on his back.

[character] RICHIE
[dialogue] Boys, you’re ruining my sunbathing 
[dialogue] vibes with all this chitter 
[dialogue] chatter.

[action] Beverly, eyes still closed, places a cigarette in her mouth 
[action] and lights up.  Ben notices her hands shake as she holds the 
[action] cigarette in her mouth.  The smoke swirls and dissipates over 
[action] them. 

[character] BEVERLY
[dialogue] Have you been seeing “things” Will?

[action] Will whips his neck to Beverly.  He can’t tell if she is 
[action] making fun or if she is seriously asking him for a bridge.

[character] WILL
[dialogue] I...  If I tell you guys something 
[dialogue] you won’t think I’m crazy.

[character] RICHIE
[dialogue] We already know you’re crazy, Will.  
[dialogue] No judgement here.

[character] WILL
[dialogue] I saw Georgie last night.  Only.  
[dialogue] It wasn’t him.

[character] EDDIE
[dialogue] Like.  In your dreams?

[character] WILL
[dialogue] No.  In my house, he tried to get 
[dialogue] me to go into the basement with 
[dialogue] him.  
[page_number] 54.

[character] RICHIE
[dialogue] That wasn’t Georgie, that was your 
[dialogue] neighborhood sex offender making a 
[dialogue] house call.

[character] WILL
[dialogue] Shut up Richie.  It wasn’t just 
[dialogue] him.  I saw this other... I don’t 
[dialogue] know.

[character] STAN
[dialogue] What did you see Will?

[character] BEN
[dialogue] The clown?

[action] Will, Stan and Eddie look at Ben.

[character] WILL
[dialogue] Yes.  That’s what it was, a scary 
[dialogue] looking clown.  And it had these 
[dialogue] teeth...

[character] STAN
[dialogue] I saw something, too.  In the 
[dialogue] basement of the synogogue.

[character] RICHIE
[dialogue] I take it back.  I’m judging all of 
[dialogue] you and you are all friggin’ 
[dialogue] wackos.

[action] Beverly, still laying there, exhales a long trail of smoke.

[character] BEVERLY
[dialogue] I need to show you guys something.  

[scene_heading] EXT. LOWER MAIN STREET - DAY

[action] The FIVE boys and Beverly stand outside her slummy apartment 
[action] building.  She looks frightened to go inside. 

[character] BEVERLY
[dialogue] My Daddy will kill me if he finds 
[dialogue] out I had boys in our apartment.

[character] BEN
[dialogue] Isn’t he at work?

[character] BEVERLY
[dialogue] Sometimes he comes home for a nap.
[page_number] 55.

[character] WILL
[dialogue] We should leave lookouts. Ben, 
[dialogue] Stan, can you?

[action] Ben is ready to protest. Then succumbs, crestfallen.

[character] STAN
[dialogue] What does he look like?

[character] BEVERLY
[dialogue] My Daddy?  Mean.  Like a drunk.

[action] Will, Richie and Eddie go with Bev.

[scene_heading] INT. BEVERLY’S APARTMENT - DAY

[action] Bev opens the door. It creeks open.  The apartment seems to 
[action] be empty.  Afternoon sunlight illuminating floating dust 
[action] motes.  She scouts around and lets them in. 

[action] Will, Richie, and Eddie follow her to a closed door at the 
[action] end of the hallway -- THE BATHROOM DOOR

[character] BEVERLY
[dialogue] In there.

[character] RICHIE
[dialogue] Can you give me an indicator of 
[dialogue] what I’m about to walk into?

[character] BEVERLY
[dialogue] You’ll see.

[character] RICHIE
[dialogue] I hope it’s Ed McMahon and I’ve 
[dialogue] just won publishers clearing house 
[dialogue] 10 million dollar sweepstakes.  But 
[dialogue] if it is, you really didn’t have to 
[dialogue] go through so much trouble, you 
[dialogue] could have just brought Ed to the 
[dialogue] quarry.

[action] She has no intention of going in. Will pushes past Richie and 
[action] opens the door.

[character] EDDIE
[dialogue] Oh God. Oh God.

[subheading] THE BLOOD

[action] Still there, faded into maroon clouds on the mirror and 
[action] wallpaper. Richie reels back into the hallway with Beverly.
[page_number] 56.

[action] She looks from Richie to Will.

[character] BEVERLY
[dialogue] You really see it?

[character] RICHIE
[dialogue] It’s like someone slaughtered a pig 
[dialogue] in here.  Or a parent.

[action] Richie is slack jawed.

[character] WILL
[dialogue] What happened?

[action] Bev is so relieved she almost cries.

[character] BEVERLY
[dialogue] The sink.  It came out from the 
[dialogue] sink.  My parents couldn’t see it. 
[dialogue] I thought I was going crazy.

[action] Will goes to Eddie, who still stands frozen in terror.

[character] WILL
[dialogue] You okay, Eddie? Eddie Kaspbrack?

[action] Eddie snaps to it, suddenly more composed, resolute even.

[character] WILL (CONT’D)
[dialogue] Ed.  Go outside.  Get Ben and send 
[dialogue] him in here.

[action] Eddie just nods and walks out the door.  

[character] WILL (CONT’D)
[dialogue] You see it, don’t you, Richie?

[action] Richie nods.  

[character] WILL (CONT’D)
[dialogue] We can’t leave it like this.  Let’s 
[dialogue] clean it up.

[scene_heading] INT. BEVERLY’S BATHROOM - DAY

[action] Will, Richie, Ben and Bev clean like grim elves, using a 
[action] bucket of hot water, ajax, and some cloth rags. Slowly the 
[action] blood washes out. Reaching for the same rag, Will and Bev’s 
[action] hands touch, a spark between them. 
[page_number] 57.

[scene_heading] EXT. BEVERLY’S APARTMENT - DAY

[action] Stan and Eddie stand lookout on a curb below.

[character] STAN
[dialogue] What’s taking them so long?

[character] EDDIE
[dialogue] There was a lot of blood.

[action] Eddie shakes his head. Something disturbing catches his 
[action] attention.

[scene_heading] INT. BEVERLY’S BATHROOM - DAY

[action] Will pours the last of pink bucket water down the bathtub 
[action] drain. The bathroom now as clean as it ever was. The bucket 
[action] is filled with blood stained rags.

[character] RICHIE
[dialogue] All I’m saying is maybe there’s 
[dialogue] some weirdo going around Derry 
[dialogue] dressed as a clown. You know, like 
[dialogue] the Joker in a “Dark Knight” comic. 
[dialogue] Some whack job who likes to dress 
[dialogue] up and kidnap kids.

[character] WILL
[dialogue] Okay, so how did he put Georgie in 
[dialogue] my basement?

[character] BEVERLY
[dialogue] Or blood in my sink?

[character] RICHIE
[dialogue] I don’t know. Who knows how crazy 
[dialogue] people do things, right?

[action] Pebbles hit the window. Richie goes over, sees Eddie and Stan 
[action] jumping up and down. The sound of footsteps come up the 
[action] hallway. They look at each other, who’s the extra footsteps?

[scene_heading] INT. STAIRS TO BEVERLY’S APARTMENT - DAY

[action] Mr. Marsh fumbles with his keys outside the door.

[scene_heading] INT. BEVERLY’S APARTMENT - DAY

[action] They race through the apartment to a window that opens to 
[action] fire escape into the alley. Bev throws it open and Will, Ben, 
[action] Bev, and Richie climb through.
[page_number] 58.

[character] WILL
[dialogue] You okay?

[character] BEVERLY
[dialogue] I am now.  Thanks to you guys.

[action] The window slams shut behind them just as Mr. Marsh enters 
[action] the hallway. 

[action] He sees a bloody rag on the counter and picks it up, wiping 
[action] his sweaty face, smearing blood all over it.

[scene_heading] INT. POLICE STATION, CHIEF BORTON’S OFFICE - DAY

[action] Chief Borton and Officer Bowers hand Mike’s mom a copy of 
[action] Mike’s signed statement.  He nearly has to defend himself 
[action] from her anger.

[] CHIEF BORTON
[action] Ma’am.  Ma’am.
[action] (BEAT)
[action] Ma’am!
[] MIKE’S MOM 
[] His father is in the hospital 
[] in critical condition and 
[] you, you’re supposed to be 
[] protecting the children!

[character] CHIEF BORTON
[dialogue] Ma’am.  We were just doing our job, 
[dialogue] but he’s free to go home with you, 
[dialogue] now.

[] MIKE’S MOM
[] He damn well better be.

[action] Officer Bowers leans into Mike.

[character] OFFICER BOWERS
[dialogue] You got lucky, boy.  Could have 
[dialogue] been you who disappeared.  Be 
[dialogue] careful next time creeping around 
[dialogue] old places like that. 

[character] MIKE
[dialogue] I told you who chased me there.

[action] Mike nods to TRAVIS BOWERS, who is sitting, shamefully, in an 
[action] office next door to Chief Borton’s office.

[character] OFFICER BOWERS
[dialogue] Oh, and he will be punished, don’t 
[dialogue] you worry, I swear on my belt 
[dialogue] buckle he will.

[action] Officer Bowers smiles, looks at Travis, whose face reveals he 
[action] knows he will get a whooping that night.
[page_number] 59.

[] MIKE’S MOM
[] C’mon, Mikey.  Your father needs 
[] us.

[action] Mike turns and follows his Mom obediently out of Chief 
[action] Borton’s office.

[scene_heading] INT. POLICE STATION, BULL PEN - SAME

[action] On their way out, Mike and his Mom pass Will, Ben, Bev, Stan, 
[action] Eddie and Richie scrambling in the door to the station. 

[character] RICHIE
[dialogue] What happened, homeschool?

[action] Mike says nothing, his eyes looking forward in anger. Will 
[action] and the others rush toward Chief Borton’s office.

[character] OFFICER BOWERS
[parenthetical] (blocking the way)
[dialogue] Whoa whoa. Where do you crumb 
[dialogue] snatchers think you’re going?

[character] WILL
[dialogue] We know what’s been taking the 
[dialogue] kids.  Chief!

[action] Borton steps out.  His long day just got worse.

[character] CHIEF BORTON
[dialogue] It’s okay, Butch.  Someone try to 
[dialogue] do something to you kids?

[character (DUAL)] BEN
[dialogue (DUAL)] Not someone.  Something.
[character (DUAL)] BEVERLY
[dialogue (DUAL)] There’s like this spirit.

[character] BEN
[dialogue] A clown.

[character] STAN
[dialogue] Or sometimes it’s something else.

[character] EDDIE
[dialogue] Only us kids can see it.

[action] Borton narrows his eyes at Richie, the only one silent.

[character] BORTON
[dialogue] Okay, stop.  Is this you Tozier?  
[dialogue] Did you put them up to this little 
[dialogue] practical joke.
[page_number] 60.

[character] WILL
[dialogue] No, we all saw it.  

[character] OFFICER BOWERS
[dialogue] Such wild imaginations.  Too bad we 
[dialogue] can’t bottle their little brains.  
[dialogue] Dry them out and grind them into 
[dialogue] powder to salt our food.  Maybe 
[dialogue] then we’d see, what was it again?

[action] Borton’s exhausted.  He’s having none of it.

[character] CHIEF BORTON
[dialogue] You youngsters realize there’s a 
[dialogue] penalty in Derry for filing a false 
[dialogue] police report.  I don’t want to 
[dialogue] have to call your parents, now.

[character] BEVERLY
[dialogue] But we’re not lying.

[character] CHIEF BORTON
[dialogue] You have any evidence?

[action] The kids all look at each other, stymied.

[character] CHIEF BORTON (CONT’D)
[dialogue] That’s what I thought.  I don’t 
[dialogue] want to hear another word of this, 
[dialogue] not here, not a whisper of it 
[dialogue] anywhere.  Now go home, all of you.  
[dialogue] Eat dinner with your families, 
[dialogue] sleep and have sweet dreams in your 
[dialogue] pj’s.  And tomorrow, you’re going 
[dialogue] to go play like normal kids and 
[dialogue] forget all about this fantasy about 
[dialogue] murder and monsters.  This is an 
[dialogue] adult matter.  You got it? 

[character] BEN
[dialogue] But you adults aren’t doing 
[dialogue] anything!

[character] OFFICER BOWERS
[dialogue] Watch your tone, boy.

[action] The kids all stand there, Borton’s big frame intimidating.

[character] CHIEF BORTON
[dialogue] I don’t see you leaving.
[page_number] 61.

[scene_heading] EXT. POLICE STATION - DAY

[action] The kids all step outside, morally deflated.

[character] WILL
[dialogue] I guess we’re on our own.

[transition] CUT TO:

[action] TITLE CARD over the PENOBSCOT RIVER: 

[] JULY 4th, 1988

[scene_heading] EXT. DERRY - AERIAL - SUNSET

[action] A smoky haze obscures the setting sun and rough hewed 
[action] mountains.  

[scene_heading] EXT. KANSAS STREET - DUSK

[action] CRRRRRACK! A mailbox explodes as a Trans-Am speeds off, the 
[action] boys inside it whooping and laughing their heads off. 

[action] The car swings up to another and Travis Bowers leans out of 
[action] the window, throwing in a SIZZLING M-80. 

[action] He shuts the lid, pulls back into the passenger seat, and 
[action] screams to the driver, Victor. 

[character] TRAVIS
[dialogue] Go! Go! Go!

[action] Victor hits it and they roar off. Snatch and MOOSE SADLER 
[dialogue] (16) scrunched like sardines in the backseat. They all look 
[action] back to see...

[action] CRRRRRACK! The mailbox explodes. The boys share a hip flask 
[action] bottle of Southern Comfort-

[character] TRAVIS (CONT’D)
[dialogue] God I love the Fourth.

[character] SNATCH
[dialogue] It’s my turn. Pass one back.
[page_number] 62.

[action] Travis fishes the bag full of M-80s and bottle rockets and 
[action] they blow another mailbox.

[scene_heading] INT. HOSPITAL ROOM - DUSK

[action] Mike enters, sheepish.  Leroy is in bed, full of tubes to 
[action] bleeping machines.  He looks decimated.  Mike’s mom is on her 
[action] way out.

[] MIKE’S MOM
[] You’ll be okay?

[action] Mike nods.  She squeezes Mike’s shoulder as she goes.

[character] LEROY
[dialogue] Mom tell you what’s the what?

[character] MIKE
[dialogue] Doctors say it’s inside your bones. 
[dialogue] And spreading.

[character] LEROY
[dialogue] Your Mom’s going to need you to be 
[dialogue] strong, Mikey.  Help keep the 
[dialogue] abatoir running.

[action] Mike nods.

[character] MIKE
[dialogue] I’ll try.

[character] LEROY
[dialogue] You do or you don’t do. No try.

[action] Leroy coughs.  His lungs are filling with fluid.

[character] MIKE
[dialogue] It’s my fault. 

[character] LEROY
[dialogue] Stop that, Mike.  There’s no one to 
[dialogue] blame here.  It’s nature taking its 
[dialogue] course.

[character] MIKE
[dialogue] You’re coming home though, right?

[action] Leroy looks at his son, sadness etched in the lines of his 
[action] sullen face.

[character] LEROY
[dialogue] Pull up that seat.
[page_number] 63.

[action] Leroy indicates a chair next to the bed.  Mike pulls it up 
[action] and sits next to his Dad, leaning in close.

[character] LEROY (CONT’D)
[dialogue] There’s something I never told 
[dialogue] nobody.  Not even your mom. 
[dialogue] Something that I think you should 
[dialogue] know in case...

[character] MIKE
[dialogue] In case of what?

[action] He coughs again.  Mike can smell the creeping death coming 
[action] from his father’s insides.

[character] LEROY
[dialogue] When I was about your age. Me and 
[dialogue] my buddies would go to this place 
[dialogue] along the canal...

[scene_heading] EXT. THE BLACK SPOT - DAY

[action] YOUNG LEROY (13) and YOUNG DICK (13) sneak up along the rail 
[action] of the canal outside a raucous, crowded little gin joint. 
[action] Only black folks stand in line to get in, music and reverie 
[action] blaring from within.  The two boys look at all the pretty 
[action] girls and snazzy men with awe.

[scene_heading] INT. HOSPITAL ROOM - DUSK

[character] MIKE
[dialogue] Dad, I’m responsible.  You don’t 
[dialogue] need to tell me some long tale 
[dialogue] about drinking and driving or safe 
[dialogue] sex.  I’ll take care of things-

[action] Leroy squeezes Mike’s arm, frustrated.  Harder than he 
[action] thought he could squeeze.

[character] LEROY
[dialogue] I’m. Not. Done.  

[action] Leroy groans through his teeth.

[character] LEROY (CONT’D)
[dialogue] This isn’t about you, Mike.  It’s 
[dialogue] not even about me.  It’s about 
[dialogue] Derry.  This town is like poison.  

[action] He coughs again.
[page_number] 64.

[character] MIKE
[dialogue] Dad?

[character] LEROY
[dialogue] Shut up and listen to me.  There 
[dialogue] was one night, 1960. I remember 
[dialogue] because it was August and Chubby 
[dialogue] Checker just dropped “the twist”. 

[scene_heading] INT. THE BLACK SPOT - NIGHT

[action] A hot hot night, the little shack filled up to the rafters 
[action] with people dancing, twisting, enjoying themselves.  YOUNG 
[action] LEROY and YOUNG DICK push their way through the sweaty bodies 
[action] to a window. They find some floaters on the sill there and 
[action] drink them up. Out the window Leroy notices...

[subheading] MEN IN WHITE ROBES

[action] Stalk from the woods and light torches.

[scene_heading] EXT. THE BLACK SPOT - NIGHT

[action] The men in white robes -- THE MAINE LEGION OF DECENCY -- 
[action] barricade the door with a cart and light the timbers on fire.

[scene_heading] INT. THE BLACK SPOT - NIGHT

[action] Smoke starts to take over the place.  People panic, stampede 
[action] the doors.  They don’t budge.  Women, men are crushed against 
[action] the hot door as people push against them to get out. Young 
[action] Leroy among them.  He’s grabbed by his friend Dick who pushes 
[action] him out the window.

[scene_heading] EXT. BLACK SPOT - NIGHT

[action] Dick and Leroy go stumbling and coughing away from the 
[action] blazing shack, their eyes burning with smoke, blinded. They 
[action] trip over the edge into the dark waters of the CANAL.

[scene_heading] INT. HOSPITAL ROOM - NIGHT

[action] He seems to float off into a reverie.

[character] LEROY
[dialogue] Worse thing I ever seen.  And the 
[dialogue] smell, that awful smell -- all 
[dialogue] cooked flesh.  Somehow we ended up 
[dialogue] in the water.  
[page_number] 65.

[] (MORE)

[character] LEROY (CONT'D)
[dialogue] Floating in the blackness, I could 
[dialogue] still hear all them screams.  It 
[dialogue] wasn’t until I surfaced that I 
[dialogue] could see the truth of it all.  Of 
[dialogue] Derry.

[character] MIKE
[dialogue] What truth?

[character] LEROY
[dialogue] I saw something, Mikey.  Through 
[dialogue] the smoke and the burning. 
[dialogue] Something I can’t even really 
[dialogue] explain, but it was there, Dickie 
[dialogue] saw it, too.  

[character] MIKE
[dialogue] What did you see?

[action] A NURSE walks in with a chemo bag.

[character] NURSE
[dialogue] Mr. Hanlon, you ready?

[action] Mike and Leroy lock eyes. Leroy leans in, whispers so the 
[action] nurse can’t hear.

[character] LEROY
[dialogue] I saw what was really responsible 
[dialogue] for that fire, Mikey.  Not the 
[dialogue] Legion.  See those white boys, they 
[dialogue] were there, but there was something 
[dialogue] else, orchestrating ‘em.  Had ‘em 
[dialogue] all in a fit and frenzy, moved to 
[dialogue] his whims.  This thing, I don’t 
[dialogue] even know how to describe it...

[scene_heading] EXT. CANAL BESIDE THE BLACK SPOT - NIGHT 

[action] A balloon surfaces from the waters, bringing with it 
[action] PENNYWISE.  It pulls him over to a survivor swimming in 
[action] darkness and Pennywise pounces, dragging the victim under 
[action] water.

[action] Leroy and Dick see this and are horrified.  They begin 
[action] swimming back to the edge when a balloon surfaces nearby, 
[action] again pulling Pennywise, his face smeared with blood, from 
[action] the water.

[action] It drags the clown toward them. They climb out of the canal 
[action] just as Pennywise gets within striking distance. Instead he 
[action] pounces on a woman there, dragging her under kicking and 
[action] screaming.
[page_number] 66.

[character] LEROY (CONT'D)

[action] On the shore Leroy rests in Dick’s arms, the Black Spot now 
[action] just a heap of smoldering char, the grounds littered with 
[action] burned up bodies.

[scene_heading] INT. HOSPITAL ROOM

[action] Mike looks at his father, terrified.

[character] NURSE
[dialogue] Mr. Hanlon?

[character] LEROY
[dialogue] (he grabs Mike’s shirt 
[dialogue] violently)
[dialogue] Be careful out there son. 
[dialogue] Especially by the canal. The water 
[dialogue] in Derry is no place for no one, 
[dialogue] especially a youngster like you.  

[action] The nurse hooks up the chemo bag to Leroy’s IV. She turns 
[action] towards Mike. 

[character] NURSE
[dialogue] You might want to wait outside.

[action] Mike hesitantly leaves the room as she pricks the needle into 
[action] his dad’s catheter, Leroy wincing.

[scene_heading] INT. HOSPITAL, CORRIDER - NIGHT

[action] Mike steps out into the corridor. The hospital seems eerily 
[action] dark, empty and quiet.

[action] A LIGHT STROBES down the long hall. It draws Mike to it, like 
[action] a moth to a flame. He turns the corner and sees... 

[action] A fire alarm pulled, white greasy fingerprints on the lever. 
[action] Emergency lights strobe along the walls. No sound of an 
[action] alarm. No doctors or nurses anywhere. 

[action] Suddenly, at the end of the long corridor, a SHEEP trots out 
[action] from a door, staccato in the strobing light. It stops, looks 
[action] at Mike, then trots into another door. 

[action] Mike starts after it, baffled. He approaches the door the 
[action] sheep walked into, a stream of WATER FLOODING OUT ONTO THE 
[action] FLOOR. 

[action] He stops, the water rushing past his feet. He looks back 
[action] behind him, the normally lit hallway now far far away.

[subheading] BAAA
[page_number] 67.

[action] He hears the sheep just inside the open door. He enters 
[action] through it without noticing the stencil on the glass of the 
[action] door: “ DERRY COUNTY CORONER.”

[scene_heading] INT. HOSPITAL, CORONER’S OFFICE 

[action] Mike enters a windowless bunker like room with all the metal 
[action] corpse lockers, metal autopsy table and a sink in the corner, 
[action] the emergency lights still strobing.

[action] The sheep is nowhere to be found.

[action] Instead Mike finds, in the middle of the floor under the 
[action] autopsy table, a DRAIN GURGLING UP WATER, the source of the 
[action] flooding out into the corridor.

[action] SLAM!

[action] The door slams behind him. Mike runs over to it, jiggles the 
[action] lock, but it won’t open. Behind him, in between flashes of 
[action] the strobe, PENNYWISE APPEARS THEN DISAPPEARS...

[action] Mike stops dead, sensing It’s presence like he did at the old 
[action] Ironworks. He slowly looks back as...

[action] ONE OF THE CORPSE LOCKER DOORS CREAK OPEN

[action] Mike opens his mouth to scream but nothing comes out.

[action] He’s pounding on the door.

[character] MIKE
[dialogue] Somebody!  HELP! Let me out! 
[parenthetical] (beat)
[dialogue] Dad!  Mom!

[action] He looks over his shoulder while continuing to BANG.  The 
[action] tray inside the open locker slowly slides out, like a tongue 
[action] from a mocking black mouth. On it, under a white sheet, is a 
[action] CORPSE.

[action] Mike dares look back just as the sheet catches and the corpse 
[action] is uncovered. It’s none other than PATRICK HOCKSTETTLER, 
[action] whose legs and arms (up to the elbow) have been bit off, 
[action] smaller, CHILD SIZE bite marks cover his body.

[action] Mike jumps to a corner where he grabs a saw to defend 
[action] himself. 

[action] Water spews up from the drain with more and more pressure, a 
[action] pool now covering the floor, the edge of which seeps toward 
[action] Mike.
[page_number] 68.

[action] Mike climbs up on a chair, afraid of the water, now with a 
[action] full view of Patrick’s mutilated body.

[action] JUST THEN PATRICK TURNS HIS HEAD AND LOOKS AT MIKE

[character] PATRICK
[dialogue] It’s killing your daddy, Mikey. 
[dialogue] Eating him away.

[character] MIKE
[dialogue] Shut up.

[character] PATRICK
[dialogue] A worm inside a rotten apple.  
[dialogue] We’re all apples, Mikey. All of us. 
[dialogue] You know the thing about apples --

[character] MIKE
[dialogue] Stop it.

[action] Patrick’s expression turns into a horrific, fearful murmur.

[character] PATRICK
[dialogue] -- they float.

[character] MIKE
[dialogue] No!

[action] THE WALLS START TO SLIDE IN.  As if the room were shrinking.  
[action] The walls shepherding him towards Patrick’s talking corpse.

[action] Mike reaches behind him, scraping at the walls, looking for 
[action] anything with purchase.  His hands find the FIRE ALARM.  
[action] Pulls it.  

[action] This time the alarm SOUNDS and the strobe lights freeze, the 
[action] room now fully lit.

[action] Mike looks back, calipers raised, and sees the locker doors 
[action] closed again, no water on the floor. 

[action] The door opens behind him. It’s the CORONER, Chief Borton, 
[action] and PATRICK’S PARENTS there to identify the remains of their 
[action] boy.

[action] Mike is nearly catatonic.  He holds onto the Chief, catching 
[action] his breath.

[character] CHIEF BORTON
[dialogue] What are you doing here, son?

[action] Speechless and terrified Mike races past them out of the 
[action] hospital.
[page_number] 69.

[scene_heading] EXT. OLD CAPE STREET - NIGHT

[action] Mike walks home, distraught, mindless to the traffic.  He is 
[action] nearly hit by a car as he storms away from the hospital.

[action] A Trans Am turns a corner behind him.  Headlights on high-
[action] beam. It stops.

[action] Mike turns, sees, realizes who it is, runs.

[scene_heading] INT. TRANS AM - SAME

[action] Travis climbs out of the passenger seat and over to the 
[action] driver seat.

[character] TRAVIS
[dialogue] Move.

[action] Victor gets out and runs around the car.  

[character] SNATCH
[dialogue] Hey, ain’t that the little shit 
[dialogue] your dad gave you a whoopin’ over?

[action] Furious, Travis slams on the gas pedal.  POV of CAR, giving 
[action] chase to Mike.

[character] TRAVIS
[dialogue] I’m gonna run him over.

[scene_heading] EXT. DERRY SIDE STREET - SAME

[action] Mike runs for his life, jumping fences, turning corners, and 
[action] running through pedestrians.

[action] Travis’ Trans-Am can’t always speed, due to the amount of 
[action] people on the street.

[action] Just when he’s about to get within biting distance, BOOM.

[action] The first of a long series of FIRE-WORKS, explodes over the 
[action] Derry sky, illuminating the street in a series of FLASHES.

[scene_heading] EXT. BEN’S HOUSE, ROOFTOP - NIGHT

[action] Will, Richie, Stan, and Eddie have gathered on Ben’s roof to 
[action] watch the fireworks.
[page_number] 70.

[action] BOOM.  The boys “oooh” and “aaaah” while stuffing their faces 
[action] with an unholy array of massed snacks meant to rot teeth and 
[action] discolor the fingers with multi-colored MSG filled flavor 
[action] powders.

[action] Richie, with sticky fingers, picks up a walkie-talkie.

[character] RICHIE
[dialogue] Benny-boy, don’t forget the ranch 
[dialogue] dressing.

[action] The walkie-talkie SQWUAKS back.

[character] BEN
[dialogue] Hey Richie.  Eat a bag of dicks.

[action] BOOM.  Again, the sky erupts in dazzling light and color AND 
[action] CONTINUES TO with fireworks THROUGHOUT AND UNTIL THE END OF 
[action] THE FOLLOWING SCENES -- IMAGINE IT AS THE MOST EPIC FIREWORKS 
[action] DISPLAY YOU HAVE EVER SEEN.

[action] Beverly, then Ben, appear from one of the dormer windows 
[action] leading to Ben’s bedroom, with sparklers, bunches of bottle 
[action] rockets, roman candles and small firecrackers.  

[character] RICHIE
[dialogue] (mimicking Paul Hogan)
[dialogue] You call that a firecracker?  This 
[dialogue] is a firecracker.

[action] He opens his palms, revealing SEVERAL M-80s.  The boys 
[action] collectively ‘woah’ with excitement.

[character] BEVERLY
[dialogue] Nice stick, Richie.  How about 
[dialogue] this.

[action] She opens her backpack, revealing a MORTAR with SIX turrets.

[character] ALL THE BOYS
[dialogue] Holy shit!

[character] WILL
[dialogue] Think that could take “IT” down?

[character (DUAL)] STAN
[dialogue (DUAL)] How?  We don’t even know what 
[dialogue (DUAL)] it is that we’re looking for.
[character (DUAL)] EDDIE
[dialogue (DUAL)] That’s a good name for it 
[dialogue (DUAL)] actually.

[character] RICHIE
[dialogue] What?

[character] EDDIE
[dialogue] “IT.”
[page_number] 71.

[action] They nod in agreement.

[character] BEVERLY
[dialogue] I think it depends.

[action] Beverly looks up from a SPREAD OF FIREWORKS laid out on a red 
[action] white and blue Captain America towel.

[character] EDDIE
[dialogue] On what?

[character] WILL
[dialogue] What we’re scared of.  It was 
[dialogue] Georgie’s corpse for me cuz, cuz 
[dialogue] that’s what I care about.  It was a 
[dialogue] naked woman for Stan because he’s 
[dialogue] so afraid of-

[character] RICHIE
[dialogue] Getting laiiiid.

[character] STAN
[action] Only because your Mom-
[dialogue] RICHIE
[dialogue] You really want me to start 
[dialogue] talking about your Mom?  
[dialogue] Don’t get me started cause it 
[dialogue] won’t stop.

[character] WILL
[dialogue] Back to point.  It showered Beverly 
[dialogue] in blood cuz...

[character] RICHIE
[dialogue] Cuz blood always freaks a girl out.

[character] BEVERLY
[dialogue] Wow, you really don’t know shit 
[dialogue] about girls do you?

[action] Will smiles.

[character] BEVERLY (CONT’D)
[dialogue] “IT” showered me in blood because I 
[dialogue] just got my period... and...

[action] Eddie recoils in disgust while Beverly’s mind goes to dark 
[action] places, to her father, and what being a “woman” might mean to 
[action] him now.  Ben and Will both notice this change in her 
[action] demeanor.

[character] RICHIE
[dialogue] I didn’t need to know that.
[page_number] 72.

[character] WILL
[dialogue] It.  It’s everything and anything. 
[dialogue] All our nightmares.  All evil, 
[dialogue] wrapped into one.

[character] BEN
[dialogue] Into one freaky looking clown you 
[dialogue] mean.  That’s the only form that 
[dialogue] seems to repeat.

[character] RICHIE
[dialogue] Ironic right?  Something that’s 
[dialogue] suppose to make kids laugh, 
[dialogue] actually being super freaky.

[action] He does it Rick James style. Everyone laughs.

[character] EDDIE
[dialogue] It makes sense though.  If it’s 
[dialogue] killing kids.  A clown is the 
[dialogue] perfect cover.

[character] BEVERLY
[dialogue] Adults just ignore them.  But kids, 
[dialogue] like your brother, Georgie...

[character] WILL
[dialogue] They want to get closer.  See their 
[dialogue] tricks.

[action] Just then Will sees MIKE running for his life down the 
[action] street.

[character] WILL (CONT’D)
[dialogue] Shit. Isn’t that-

[character] RICHIE
[dialogue] Home school.

[action] They look where he’s running from and see Bowers Gang, in the 
[action] Trans-Am delayed by foot traffic.

[character] EDDIE
[dialogue] They’re going to cream him.

[character] STAN

[dialogue] for a night.

[character] BEVERLY
[dialogue] That’s cowardly, Stan.
[page_number] 73.

[character] BEN

[dialogue] hide from them.  We need to do 
[dialogue] something.

[character] STAN
[dialogue] How?

[character] WILL
[dialogue] With these.

[action] Will points to their arsenal of fireworks.

[transition] CUT TO:

[scene_heading] EXT. BEN’S NEIGHBORHOOD - MINUTES LATER

[action] Mike runs past families and kids distracted by the fireworks 
[action] display happening overhead, Bowers gang almost on him in the 
[action] Trans Am.

[action] Mike cuts between a lawn between two houses, hopping a fence. 
[action] Bowers hits the gas and fishtails it around the next block to 
[action] cut Mike off.

[action] Will and his gang see this and take a short cut of their own. 
[action] Ben, boy-boobs jiggling, takes up the rear.

[scene_heading] EXT.  NEIBOLT STREET - MOMENTS LATER - NIGHT

[action] Mike spills out between lawns on Neibolt street; a dead end.  
[action] There is a small pier that cuts into the canal with an old 
[action] abandoned “lock house” at the end. 

[action] The Trans-Am roars into view at the other end of the block.  
[action] Revs the engine.

[scene_heading] INT. TRANS AM - NIGHT

[action] Travis is focused on Mike, who is frozen in the street.

[character] TRAVIS
[dialogue] Got the little fucker now.

[action] Another boy runs out with him. Will.

[character] VICTOR
[dialogue] Is that that pussy Denbrough?
[page_number] 74.

[character] TRAVIS
[dialogue] This night just gets better and 
[dialogue] better.

[action] Travis slams on the gas.

[scene_heading] EXT. NEIBOLT STREET - NIGHT

[action] Tires screeching.  Travis guns it straight for Mike and Will.

[character] WILL
[dialogue] Don’t move. 

[character] MIKE
[dialogue] You nuts?

[character] WILL
[dialogue] Trust us.  We’re here to help you 
[dialogue] take these jerks.

[character] MIKE
[dialogue] We?

[action] Looking to the side, Will has his walkie-talkie.

[character] WILL
[dialogue] (into the walkie-talkie)
[dialogue] Not yet... not yet. Now!

[action] Between the Trans-Am and Mike and Will, Ben and Richie heave 
[action] their shoulders into a large steel DUMPSTER filled with 
[action] construction material.  They push it down a driveway that 
[action] leads to the street.  It creaks, starts to wobble, then move 
[action] down the incline.  When it hits street level, though, the 
[action] gutter stops it from going out into line of the Trans Am.

[character] BEN
[dialogue] Fuck.

[action] Will sees this.

[character] WILL
[dialogue] Oh shit. Abort! Abort!

[character] MIKE
[dialogue] Abort? What does that mean.

[character] WILL
[dialogue] Run!

[action] They’re about to dodge the car when-
[page_number] 75.

[action] BEVERLY jumps out firing off her 6-BARRELED MORTAR handheld, 
[action] aimed horizontally, at the car.  THUMP.  The recoil throws 
[action] her back while the rocketing projectile explodes in front of 
[action] the Trans-Am.  Travis and the boys shield their faces 
[action] SCREAMING as the front windshield explodes.  

[character] BEVERLY
[dialogue] Woah!

[action] THUMP.  This one knocks her to the ground as the second 
[action] mortar flies right towards the Trans Am.  Travis yanks the 
[action] wheel to the LEFT as the firework EXPLODES on the side of the 
[action] car.  He slams on the breaks as Victor, Snatch, and Moose are 
[action] flung forward.  Victor smashes his face on the dash board.

[character] MIKE
[dialogue] Is that girl for real?

[character] WILL
[dialogue] Real as it gets... Now, Stan and 
[dialogue] Eddie!

[action] Eddie and Stan charge from behind a hedge.  Lighting bottle-
[action] rockets aimed at the car.  Travis and his boys have to duck 
[action] for cover as ONE of the rockets ZINGS through the car.

[character] TRAVIS
[dialogue] Those little motherfuckers.  Get 
[dialogue] out, get out!

[action] The OLDER BOYS climb out the windows, ducking and covering 
[action] their eyes from the ONSLAUGHT of bottle rockets and roman 
[action] candle projectiles.

[action] Crouched behind the trunk, Travis lights up three M-80’s WITH 
[action] CLOWN FACES on them and tosses them like grenades at Beverly, 
[action] Stan, and Eddie’s positions. 

[action] The M-80’s blow HUGE fist sized holes in the blacktop, bigger 
[action] than can possibly be legal, exploding gravel peppering the 
[action] kids, stunning them for a moment.

[character] STAN
[dialogue] Holy hell. What kinda gear do they 
[dialogue] got?

[character] WILL
[dialogue] I don’t wanna stay and find out.

[action] Ben and Richie race up from behind, letting loose another 
[action] barrage as they pass Bowers’ gang and the Trans Am, giving 
[action] cover as the FIRE FIGHT, literally, becomes a retreating 
[action] manoeuver towards the pier at the end of Neibolt street. 
[page_number] 76.

[action] Travis’ boys cover their eyes but they make aggressive 
[action] progress towards the kids with their terrifying M-80’s.

[character] TRAVIS
[dialogue] I’ve been waiting all summer to run 
[dialogue] into you, Denbrough. 

[character] WILL
[dialogue] Leave us alone Bowers.  I don’t 
[dialogue] want anyone to get seriously hurt.

[character] TRAVIS
[dialogue] Hurt?  When I’m done with you, 
[dialogue] you’ll wish you were in that storm 
[dialogue] drain with your brother.

[scene_heading] EXT. NEIBOLT STREET PIER - CONTINUOUS

[action] They regroup at the mouth of the pier, water on both sides, 
[action] Bowers in front. He throws another M-80, which tears into a 
[action] wood plank.  Splinters shoot everywhere.

[character] BEVERLY
[dialogue] Will, we need cover--

[character] BEN
[dialogue] The house. It’s our only chance!

[action] Ben points to the Neibolt Street house on stilts over the 
[action] black water. An imploded, condemned structure that slopes 
[action] like a man’s half eaten face. Eddie is instantly horrified.

[character] EDDIE
[dialogue] I don’t think that’s a good idea.

[character] RICHIE
[dialogue] When do you ever think anything is 
[dialogue] a good idea?

[character] WILL
[dialogue] Okay. Ben, we’ll hold him off while 
[dialogue] we can, you and Stan see if you can 
[dialogue] find a way in.

[action] Covering them, Mike, Richie and Will continue to fire off 
[action] what fireworks they have left. Bowers’ boys light off another 
[action] barrage of M-80’s. Will, armed with a plank bats one back 
[action] like a baseball. BOOM! It explodes at Victors feet.

[action] Ben quickly runs towards the house, followed by Stan and Bev.  

[action] Eddie, stuck in the middle, goes after them down the pier.
[page_number] 77.

[scene_heading] EXT. NEIBOLT STREET HOUSE - CONTINUOUS

[action] Ben and Stan split up looking for a way in.  All windows and 
[action] doors are boarded up or locked.

[character] EDDIE
[dialogue] Really guys, there has to be 
[dialogue] another option.

[character] RICHIE
[dialogue] You want to swim?!

[character] EDDIE
[dialogue] I’m serious. This house--

[centered] BEN, STAN, & BEVERLY
[] Shut up, Eddie.

[action] And then, as if by magic (or by Pennywise), one of the 2x4s 
[action] that cover a window to the basement slips to the ground.

[scene_heading] EXT. NEIBOLT STREET PIER - NIGHT

[action] Will finds the backpack empty as Mike shoots off the last 
[action] bottle rocket.  Will picks up some rocks as ammo.  Mike and 
[action] Richie instinctively follow suit.  Will winds up and... 

[character] WILL
[dialogue] Charge!!!

[action] NAILS MOOSE IN THE FOREHEAD, drawing blood. 

[action] Travis and the others stop dead, incredulous, as rocks pelt 
[action] them.  It turns into a crazy rock fight.  Will looks back.  
[action] He can’t see any of his friends who are on the other side of 
[action] the house.

[character] WILL (CONT’D)
[parenthetical] (to Ben)
[dialogue] Guys hurry!!!

[scene_heading] EXT. NEIBOLT STREET HOUSE - NIGHT

[action] Ben and Beverly look at the window into the blackened hole, 
[action] then at each other ominously.

[character] BEVERLY
[dialogue] Rock, paper, scissors?

[character] STAN
[dialogue] I’ll go.
[page_number] 78.

[action] Not wanting to be one-upped by Stan, Ben pushes him out of 
[action] the way.

[character] BEN
[dialogue] No.  I’ll go.

[scene_heading] EXT. NEIBOLT STREET PIER - NIGHT

[action] The fight is getting desperate.  Both Will and Mike bleed 
[action] from rock hits.  Will, Richie, and Mike see Bev waving them 
[action] to come.  They run for their lives towards the house, Travis 
[action] and his goons on their heels.  

[scene_heading] EXT. NEIBOLT STREET HOUSE - NIGHT

[action] Will, Richie and Mike round the corner of the house.  Will 
[action] looks down the pier, no one, then THEY HEAR BEN.  

[character] BEN
[parenthetical] (whispering loudly)
[dialogue] Down here!

[character] TRAVIS (O.C.)
[dialogue] I’m gonna kill you, losers! I swear 
[dialogue] I’m gonna kill you all.

[action] Will sees a broomstick wave in the opening, beckoning them to 
[action] come in.

[character] WILL
[dialogue] Go! Go! Go!

[action] Mike and Richie slip in, lowering themselves onto a floor 
[action] littered with crates and boxes.  Will last in just as... 

[action] Travis and his buddies round the house. Moose walks right by 
[action] the window they slipped into but doesn’t seem to see it.

[character] TRAVIS
[dialogue] Where are they?

[action] Victor and Snatch look at Travis for direction.  Unmoved, 
[action] Travis walks around the boarded up house looking for a way 
[action] in.

[scene_heading] INT. NEIBOLT STREET HOUSE, BASEMENT

[action] The kids catch their breath and lick their wounds.  The 
[action] basement is a derelict and creepy undercarriage to a canal 
[action] lock house.  
[page_number] 79.

[action] A huge coal ENGINE bulked in the shadows, and next to it an 
[action] old COAL BIN and stone channel that leads directly to the 
[action] canal and water.

[character] RICHIE
[dialogue] If we die here I just want to say 
[dialogue] that fight was friggin’ awesome.

[character] WILL
[dialogue] Shhhhush.

[action] Richie does a little pistol shoot with his hands to Ben and 
[action] mouths “awesome.”  Ben smiles.

[character] EDDIE
[parenthetical] (to himself)
[dialogue] We shouldn’t be here.

[action] Will watches Travis and his boys walk past the basement 
[action] window again. Ben looks to Beverly, sees some gravel in her 
[action] leg like buckshot.

[character] BEN
[parenthetical] (whispering)
[dialogue] You okay?

[character] BEVERLY
[dialogue] It’s just a scrape.

[scene_heading] EXT. NEIBOLT STREET HOUSE

[action] Travis is getting frustrated.  He can’t find where or how the 
[action] kids have gotten into the house.  He looks straight at the 
[action] window the kids crawled through, the 2x4 is still somehow 
[action] attached from the outside.

[scene_heading] INT. NEIBOLT STREET HOUSE, BASEMENT

[action] The kids can see Travis staring straight at them through the 
[action] window, the 2x4 is non-existent, as if Pennywise is 
[action] preventing the boys outside from seeing in or entering.

[character] STAN
[dialogue] They can’t see the window?

[character] EDDIE
[parenthetical] (still repeating)
[dialogue] We shouldn’t be here.

[character] RICHIE
[dialogue] Don’t count your chickens.
[page_number] 80.

[character] MIKE

[dialogue] here?

[character] EDDIE
[dialogue] We shouldn’t be here!

[character (DUAL)] WILL
[dialogue (DUAL)] Eddie?
[character (DUAL)] RICHIE
[dialogue (DUAL)] He’s afraid of dirt.

[action] Will covers Eddie’s mouth as he continues to yell, “we 
[action] shouldn’t be here.”  They watch anxiously as Travis and the 
[action] boys feet inch uncomfortably closer to them.  

[action] Then Eddie suddenly stops.  

[scene_heading] EXT. NEIBOLT STREET HOUSE - SAME

[action] Frustrated, Travis and the boys leave.

[scene_heading] INT. NEIBOLT STREET HOUSE, BASEMENT

[action] The kids have surrounded Eddie.

[character] BEVERLY

[character] EDDIE
[dialogue] There’s something I didn’t tell you 
[dialogue] guys.

[dialogue] once.  I thought it was just my 
[dialogue] mind playing tricks, but now I 
[dialogue] know...

[action] Eddie nods.

[character] STAN
[dialogue] Jesus.
[page_number] 81.

[dialogue] guy?

[action] newest member.

[character] WILL
[dialogue] Something’s wrong with Derry.  
[dialogue] Haunting it.  Hunting and killing 
[dialogue] kids.  We’ve all seen it.

[action] Mike looks at them all, judging their earnestness.  Feels he 
[action] can trust them, an instant connection with people whose fear 
[action] clings to your own.

[character] MIKE
[dialogue] I know.  I’ve seen “IT” too.

[action] Just then, the house seems to shiver.  As if its foundation 
[action] had shifted.  A CLANKING SOUND resonates from the pipes. 
[action] Water BEGINS TO SEEP in through the cracks in the basement.

[character] BEVERLY
[dialogue] What exactly did you see here, 
[dialogue] Eddie?

[action] Ben goes to the window they came through, but instead of 
[action] seeing out, it’s now covered by the 2x4.

[character] BEN
[dialogue] Guys, uh--

[action] He looks at their feet.  They all do.  Water coming in fast.  
[action] Ben bangs a coal stoker against the boarded window.  Rock 
[action] solid.  Will looks for a way out. The stairs.

[character] WILL
[dialogue] Stairs.

[action] Suddenly they HEAR FOOTSTEPS, above.  They look up as dust 
[action] falls from one side of the roof, slowly making its way to 
[action] directly over them.  To the stairs.

[character] EDDIE
[dialogue] (whispering in fright)
[dialogue] It’s here.  
[page_number] 82.

[action] Will grabs on to Beverly’s hand.  She holds onto his with 
[action] equal vigor.  Richie holds onto Eddie. 

[action] Ben holds the stoker like a sword.  Mike grabs a shovel.  
[action] They lead the way up the stairs. 

[action] Unless otherwise noted, they ONLY WHISPER now.

[character] RICHIE
[dialogue] I’m not going up there.

[action] The others ignore him, follow Ben and Mike up the stairs.  

[action] Richie looks around him at the scary basement, the furnace 
[action] hulking in the corner like a being with eyes. The water 
[action] surrounds his shoes. He runs up after his friends.

[scene_heading] INT. NEIBOLT STREET HOUSE, 1ST FLOOR

[action] A door opens in the middle of the kind of creepy hallway you 
[action] don’t want to find yourself in, ever.  Scummy yellow water 
[action] stained wallpaper peeling like dead skin from walls.  Sconces 
[action] hanging from their sockets and detritus is built up on the 
[action] wooden floor boards.  No footprints anywhere.

[action] Ben sticks his head out.  He looks left, nothing, he looks 
[action] right, nothing.  Directly in front of him, through a dark 
[action] parlor, is the FRONT DOOR.

[action] As Ben disappears into the stairs again, the door closes, 
[action] revealing PENNYWISE THE CLOWN, at the far end of the hallway.

[scene_heading] INT. NEIBOLT STREET HOUSE, BASEMENT STAIRS

[action] Ben still has his hand on the door knob.

[character] BEN
[dialogue] The front door is right there.  

[action] Will nods.  Let’s go.

[scene_heading] INT. NEIBOLT STREET HOUSE, 1ST FLOOR

[action] Pennywise is gone.

[action] The kids all file out, sticking close to the filthy walls.  
[action] They dart into the dark parlor for the door, followed last by 
[action] Richie...  

[action] The room seems to extend and last longer than anticipated.  
[action] Finally they get to the door.  Ben tries it.  Nailed shut.
[page_number] 83.

[character] EDDIE
[dialogue] Shit. Guys, look.

[action] Everyone turns and sees HUNDREDS OF CROWS perched atop the 
[action] railing of the main staircase and in the rafters above. 

[character] MIKE
[dialogue] There must be a window open.

[action] Will reads his mind, an upstairs window might be their only 
[action] chance to freedom.

[character] WILL
[dialogue] C’mon.

[action] Will leads the way upstairs.

[scene_heading] INT. NEIBOLT STREET HOUSE, 2ND FLOOR

[action] The children, petrified, slide up the stairs as silently as 
[action] possible, trying not to disturb the watching crows.  Or alert 
[action] whatever else is there.  The stairs creak and snap loudly.  

[action] They get to a 2nd floor hallway, the wind from an open window 
[action] blowing trash around.  Will looks around...

[scene_heading] INT. NEIBOLT STREET HOUSE, 2ND FLOOR BEDROOM

[action] He beckons them into a bedroom with AN OPEN WINDOW.  They 
[action] step over a moldy bare mattress shoved against the wall.  

[action] Beverly is the first one out the window, then Stan, then 
[action] Richie, then Ben.  Ben looks back and sees Eddie standing by 
[action] the doorway to the hallway.

[character] BEN
[dialogue] Eddie, what are you doing?

[action] Eddie turns towards them, realizes he must have lost 
[action] consciousness for a second, doesn’t understand why he was 
[action] standing there.  He walks towards the window when 

[action] THE FLOOR GIVES WAY and he tumbles down to the first floor.

[scene_heading] INT. NEIBOLT STREET HOUSE, 1ST FLOOR, KITCHEN

[action] If the house weren’t spooky enough, this room is the 
[action] spookiest.  A bunch of furniture is gathered in the middle of 
[action] the room covered in sheets.  A dusty old refrigerator stands 
[action] in the corner.  Dust everywhere begins to clear from Eddie’s 
[action] fall.  He is knocked out. His arm is clearly broken.
[page_number] 84.

[action] down.  Mike and Ben shore themselves behind him.

[dialogue] when you’re afraid.
