[scene_heading] INT. NEIBOLT STREET HOUSE, 2ND FLOOR

[action] Mike lifts up the mattress and sees a decomposed corpse of 
[action] some child underneath it.  He screams, falling backwards 
[action] towards Will and Ben. 

[scene_heading] INT. NEIBOLT STREET HOUSE, KITCHEN

[action] SUDDENLY ALL THREE OF THEM

[action] Fall onto Pennywise, smacking into the floor, blood 
[action] splattering the walls, impact cushioned by Pennywise’s body.  
[action] They instantly fall to the side. 

[action] Will, Mike and Ben look over to see what broke their fall- a 
[action] just as startled Pennywise, writhing in anger.

[character] WILL
[dialogue] Ah, fuck!

[action] Eddie scurries away from Pennywise, who bares his fangs at 
[action] Will.

[action] CRASH!

[action] Just then the board from the window rips off to Richie, Stan 
[action] and Beverly.

[action] She fires a bottle rocket at Pennywise’s face, exploding 
[action] right into his eye.  The clown reels back howling.

[action] Richie and Ben jump in and grab Eddie. 

[action] Pennywise pulls the rocket out of his burned socket and 
[action] growls at the boys, morphing into an ORANGE GAS that shoots 
[action] back into the fridge.

[action] Eddie, Ben and Richie climb to safety through the window, 
[action] while Will watches from the window frame Pennywise’s escape.
[page_number] 88.

[scene_heading] EXT. EDDIE KASPBRAK’S HOUSE - NIGHT

[action] The kids BANG on Mrs. Kaspbrak’s door. She opens it, sees his 
[action] broken arm and freaks.

[scene_heading] EXT. EDDIE KASPBRAK’S HOUSE - MOMENTS LATER

[action] Mrs. Kaspbrak is placing Eddie in the front seat of their 
[action] family car.  He is delirious with pain.

[character] MRS. KASPBRAK
[dialogue] You! You all did this!

[action] She slams the door shut, hurting Eddie on the inside.  The 
[action] kids are all too spooked to talk coherently. 

[character] MRS. KASPBRAK (CONT’D)
[dialogue] You know how delicate he is!

[character] RICHIE
[dialogue] We were attacked, Mrs. K. Travis 
[dialogue] Bowers and--

[character] MRS. KASPBRAK
[dialogue] Stop it! Just stop it!  If it 
[dialogue] weren’t for you boys and that 
[dialogue] little tramp they’d have nothing 
[dialogue] against my Eddie-bear... Ohh, where 
[dialogue] are my keys?

[action] She’s nearly in tears searching her purse.  She dumps it out 
[action] on the ground, prescription bottles, used kleenex’s, food, 
[action] deflated balloons.  Gets on her knees and picks through it.

[character] MRS. KASPBRAK (CONT’D)
[dialogue] Eddie’s done with you kids, you 
[dialogue] hear?

[action] She gets up when she finds the car key, speaking with such 
[action] vitriolic focus, she seems possessed.

[character] MRS. KASPBRAK (CONT’D)
[dialogue] Don’t come around here again, you 
[dialogue] don’t deserve him. You’re reckless, 
[dialogue] churlish little boys.
[parenthetical] (to Will)
[dialogue] This is why your little brother 
[dialogue] died, William, because you are a 
[dialogue] LOSER, a reckless reckless reckless 
[dialogue] loser.  God help me if I were one 
[dialogue] of your parents.
[page_number] 89.

[action] She gets in the car and screeches away towards the hospital. 
[action] They ALL watch the car disappear down the street.

[character] RICHIE
[dialogue] Well, that went well.

[character] BEN
[dialogue] Guys, I hate to pile on bad news.

[action] Ben noses up to the window, Mrs. Kaspbrack’s TV set flashing 
[action] in the dark.  They all nose up, see what Ben sees. 

[character] STAN
[dialogue] No.

[action] ON THE LOCAL NEWS: Chief Borton and Officer Bowers pose in 
[action] front of camera with THE ONE-ARMED OLD MAN in the U.S.S. 
[action] Indianapolis hat in handcuffs. Caption under them reads:

[action] “CHILD SERIAL KILLER APPREHENDED BY LOCAL POLICE. DERRY 
[action] CITIZENRY BREATHS A SIGH OF RELIEF.”  

[character] BEVERLY
[dialogue] That can’t be true.  We just saw 
[dialogue] “IT.”

[action] Mike just shakes his head.

[character] MIKE
[dialogue] This is all crazy.  Who are you 
[dialogue] guys anyway? 

[character] BEN
[dialogue] The only losers in town-

[character] WILL
[dialogue] Who know what’s going on. 

[character] RICHIE
[dialogue] Welcome to the club, kid.

[action] They half-heartedly pat Mike on the back. 

[transition] CUT TO:

[action] TITLE CARD OVER THE LAST SLIVER OF LIGHT BEYOND DERRY’S 
[action] MOUNTAIN RANGE.
[page_number] 90.

[character] AUGUST

[action] Tilt down to the OVERHEAD shot of what we expect to be “Derry 
[action] 1988”, but instead we see forests, logging trails, a 
[action] hardscrabble 19th century downtown, and a LOG CABIN SALOON 
[action] squatting on the edge of the swollen Kenduskeag canal. Smoke 
[action] billows from its chimney, day drawing down to a misty dark.

[scene_heading] EXT. SILVER DOLLAR SALOON - CONTINUOUS

[action] Follow from behind, a MAN in a lumberjack’s jacket walking 
[action] through the muddy, waterlogged street, a double-bited ax 
[action] slung over his shoulder. 

[action] A WOMAN pleads with the Sheriff on the side of the street.

[character] WOMAN
[dialogue] He didn’t run away, sir!  He’s just 
[dialogue] a child, missing now for more than 
[dialogue] three days!

[action] She sees the MAN.

[character] WOMAN (CONT’D)
[parenthetical] (to man)
[dialogue] Have you seen my baby, Claude?  
[dialogue] He’s been gone near...

[action] Claude ignores the woman... 

[action] Chryon:
[] 1879

[action] And walks into the saloon, “SILVER DOLLAR” carved deep and 
[action] poorly into the wood over the door.

[scene_heading] INT. SILVER DOLLAR SALOON - NIGHT

[action] Dozens of loggers are bellied up at the bar, drinking beers, 
[action] spitting chew on the sawdust covered floor. The man with the 
[action] ax, CLAUDE HEROUX, elbows himself a place at the bar and 
[action] throws down a TWO DOLLAR BILL.
[page_number] 91.

[action] The bartender brings Heroux a schooner of beer, one 
[action] hardboiled egg in a bowl, a shaker of salt and some change. 
[action] Heroux salts the egg and the beer, his piercing eyes stuck on

[action] A CARD GAME AT A TABLE OF 5 MEN IN BACK

[action] He devours the egg, downs the beer, then lets out a belch. 
[action] The man next to him gives a friendly nod. Heroux doesn’t 
[action] respond. He kicks back from the bar, dragging his ax behind.

[action] Heroux walks past a piano being played, whispers something 
[action] into the players ear, then throws the change from his meal -- 
[action] $1.85 -- onto the piano top. The piano player tips his hat 
[action] and changes tune to something more festive, manic almost.

[action] Heroux continues into the back, walking like a man in a half 
[action] dream. 

[action] At the table, EL KATOOK deals out a hand to a group of 
[action] railroad bulls -- TINKER MCCUTCHEON, FLOYD CALDERWOOD, 
[action] “STUGLEY” GRENIER, and EDDIE KING, a bearded man whose 
[action] spectacles are almost as fat as his gut. 

[character] FLOYD CALDERWOOD
[dialogue] You sitting in Claude?

[action] Floyd reaches out to grab his hand when...

[action] THUNK!

[action] Heroux’s ax falls, chopping of Floyd’s hand at the wrist. 
[action] Floyd recoils back shrieking, blood spurting from the stump, 
[action] his severed hand remaining on the table.

[action] At the bar somebody calls for more beer. The bartender takes 
[action] a casual look back just in time to see...

[action] Heroux bury his ax in Tinker McCutchoen’s head. The big man 
[action] tries to get up, blood pouring down his face, then sits back 
[action] down again. A second chop finishes him.

[action] Floyd, writhing under the table, gropes for his hand on the 
[action] table above it. Heroux chops his other hand off, Floyd 
[action] screams even louder. 

[action] Laughter from the bar as patrons rub the bartender’s bald 
[action] head for luck. The piano playing only gets more giddy and 
[action] festive, then we see who’s playing it: 

[action] PENNYWISE, fingers dancing across the keys like a virtuoso.

[action] Heroux turns to Ed Katook, who struggles to pull his clutch 
[action] pistol from its shoulder holster. He slings the ax sideways 
[action] just as Katook gets the pistol out, burying it in his back.
[page_number] 92.

[action] CRACK!

[action] A shot goes off. But at the bar, conversation between two 
[action] loggers turns to the weather. This winter will be a jeezer.

[action] Fat Eddie King falls to the floor trying to crab away from 
[action] the table. Heroux, bloody ax dragging on the floor, comes 
[action] over to the fat man who pleads.

[character] EDDIE KING
[dialogue] Please Claude, I just got married 
[dialogue] last month.

[action] The ax comes down into Eddie’s ample gut. Blood sprays all 
[action] the way up to the Dollar’s beamed roof. 

[action] Men at the bar laugh and eat their boiled eggs, dully aware 
[action] of the gorefest behind them. 

[action] Heroux pulls the ax out of his gut like you would a softwood 
[action] tree, rocking it back and forth. It finally comes free and he 
[action] brings it down again.

[action] THWAP!

[action] Eddie’s severed head goes rolling over toward the bar, right 
[action] to the foot of a drunk, who without breaking his call for 
[action] another drink, kicks it away.

[action] In back, Heroux turns to the last man cowering in the corner: 
[action] Stugley Grenier. He takes a swing and Stugley ducks it, ax 
[action] splintering the floor.

[action] Stugley scrambles into the OUTHOUSE and closes the door 
[action] behind. Heroux, now totally slicked with blood, stalks over 
[action] and begins chopping his way in.

[action] He finds the windowless room empty. A sound comes from deep 
[action] in the pooping hole. He looks down just in time to see...

[action] Stugley’s boots disappear through the pool of shit and piss.

[action] Heroux steps out of the outhouse into the carnage of the back 
[action] room. Exhausted and bloody from tip to toe, he takes a seat 
[action] at the card table, props his ax next to him, and starts 
[action] drinking what’s left of the slaughtered men’s beers.

[action] No one in the saloon seems to pay much mind to this. No one 
[action] except Pennywise at the piano, who takes great pleasure in 
[action] the scene and the ditty he’s playing, a greasy white smile on 
[action] his giddy, evil face...
[page_number] 93.

[scene_heading] INT. BEN’S HOUSE, BEDROOM - DAY

[action] The Loser’s Club sits in front of Ben.

[character] BEN
[dialogue] And that is the story of the Silver 
[dialogue] Dollar Saloon.

[action] They look horrified.

[character] RICHIE
[dialogue] The point of telling us was?

[action] Ben looks at Will.

[character (DUAL)] WILL
[dialogue (DUAL)] I think it’s pretty obvious.
[character (DUAL)] BEVERLY
[dialogue (DUAL)] Derry’s not just haunted, 
[dialogue (DUAL)] it’s cursed.

[character] MIKE
[dialogue] Just like my pa was trying to warn 
[dialogue] me the night I met you guys.

[character] BEN
[dialogue] That clown, whatever “IT” is, has 
[dialogue] been around Derry doing nasty 
[dialogue] things for a long long time.  The 
[dialogue] Black Spot fire.  The Bradley Gang 
[dialogue] massacre. All the way back to the 
[dialogue] first settlers who disappeared.

[action] Richie holds up a dog-eared copy of “A History of Old Derry”.

[character (DUAL)] RICHIE
[dialogue (DUAL)] I don’t know, man.  That 
[dialogue (DUAL)] flapjack story--
[character (DUAL)] STAN
[dialogue (DUAL)] Lumberjack.

[character] RICHIE
[dialogue] Whatevs.  It sounds like bunk to 
[dialogue] me.  Didn’t the guy who wrote this 
[dialogue] go insane or something?

[character] WILL
[dialogue] Exactly.  No one listened to him 
[dialogue] like no one listens to us.  And 
[dialogue] unless we do something, “IT’s” 
[dialogue] never going to stop.

[character] STAN
[dialogue] What do you mean, us, Will?  We’re 
[dialogue] just kids.
[page_number] 94.

[character] BEVERLY
[dialogue] We hurt it at Neibolt.  That’s 
[dialogue] gotta mean something.

[character] MIKE
[dialogue] Everything dies.

[action] Richie looks at Mike, rolls his eyes.

[character] WILL
[dialogue] We can kill it.

[character] RICHIE
[dialogue] Okay Schwarzenegger twins, simmer 
[dialogue] down. We can’t even free Eddie from 
[dialogue] “mom jail” let alone take down some 
[dialogue] freaky shape-shifter with a mind-
[dialogue] meld on all the adults of Derry.  
[dialogue] However, I do believe we could sell 
[dialogue] this story to “Tales from the 
[dialogue] crypt.” 

[character (DUAL)] BEN
[dialogue (DUAL)] No, no.  This is far too 
[dialogue (DUAL)] whacked for “Tales.”
[character (DUAL)] STAN
[dialogue (DUAL)] I don’t want to see “it” 
[dialogue (DUAL)] again.  Ever.

[character] WILL
[dialogue] I don’t think that’s a choice, 
[dialogue] Stan.  “IT” knows we know.  We hurt 
[dialogue] it.  It’s going to want to hurt us 
[dialogue] back. 

[character] MIKE
[dialogue] It’s only a matter of time one of 
[dialogue] us is found half eaten in the 
[dialogue] Barrens.  Or worse.

[action] This is too much for Stan. He grabs his backpack.

[character] STAN
[dialogue] I need to study for my bar-mitzvah.

[action] Stan goes. All the kids look to each other.

[character] BEVERLY
[dialogue] He’s just scared.

[character] WILL
[dialogue] We’re all scared.  That’s what it 
[dialogue] feeds off of.  I heard the clown 
[dialogue] tell Eddie as much.  Our only 
[dialogue] defense is to stick together.
[page_number] 95.

[character] RICHIE
[dialogue] Yeah well, the only time I’ve been 
[dialogue] spooked is when I’m with you 
[dialogue] jokers.  I’m thinking I might be 
[dialogue] safer at home.  At least I know my 
[dialogue] parents won’t try to kill me.

[action] Richie goes too, leaving just the four.

[scene_heading] INT. WILL’S HOUSE, KITCHEN - DAY

[action] Will enters the back door and grabs Stan’s invitation for his 
[action] Bar-Mitzvah off the fridge:

[centered] HAROLD AND RUTH URIS

[centered] TO 
[centered] THE BAR-MITZVAH OF THEIR SON 
[centered] STANLEY URIS 
[centered] ON 
[centered] SATURDAY, AUGUST 13TH, 1988

[action] He hears fighting in the hallway.

[character] SHARON
[dialogue] Put it back up there! Put it back 
[dialogue] now!

[character] ZACH
[dialogue] I just thought, someone else--

[scene_heading] INT. WILL’S HOUSE, HALLWAY - CONTINUOUS

[action] His mother stands in the way of his father who holds the 
[action] chest of Georgie's toys. 

[character] SHARON
[dialogue] I don’t care about anyone else. I 
[dialogue] care about Georgie. I care about my 
[dialogue] little boy who’s gone.

[character] WILL
[dialogue] Hey.

[action] They both startle and look at Will standing there.

[character] ZACH
[dialogue] Willy, we need a minute--

[character] WILL
[dialogue] Why? To scream at each other more?
[page_number] 96.

[character] SHARON
[dialogue] This is between your father and me.

[character] WILL
[dialogue] And Georgie right? You, dad, and 
[dialogue] Georgie.

[character] ZACH
[dialogue] Willy, stop.

[character] WILL
[dialogue] No. You stop. I hate it. Tip toeing 
[dialogue] around you guys like I don’t exist. 
[dialogue] Like I’m the one who died and the 
[dialogue] only one who’s still here is Ge--

[action] SLAP!

[action] His mother hits him hard. She instantly regrets it. She runs 
[action] down the stairs sobbing. Zach reaches out a comforting hand.

[character] ZACH
[dialogue] You okay?

[character] WILL
[dialogue] What do you care? At least she 
[dialogue] heard me.

[character] ZACH
[dialogue] Son--

[character] WILL
[dialogue] I miss Georgie, too.  

[character] ZACH
[dialogue] We know.

[character] WILL
[dialogue] Do you?  Because it seems like 
[dialogue] every adult in this town is blind 
[dialogue] to what’s really happening, blind 
[dialogue] to what any of us kids are going 
[dialogue] through.

[action] Will shoulders past his father and runs out the door.

[scene_heading] EXT. CORNER OF WITCHAM AND JACKSON - DAY

[action] Will sits on the curb across from the storm drain that killed 
[action] Georgie. His eyes burn with anger.  He stares into the dark 
[action] void, half expecting Pennywise’s face to appear any moment.
[page_number] 97.

[character] WILL
[dialogue] C’mon, clown. Show your ugly face! 
[dialogue] I’ll stick another firecracker in 
[dialogue] it for Georgie. Blow your whole 
[dialogue] stupid head off. Send you back to 
[dialogue] wherever a creeper like you comes 
[dialogue] from. Where are you!? 

[action] He listens for a response.  Nothing.  Just the bubbling sound 
[action] of water rushing through the sewer.  Water.  Something dawns 
[action] on Will.

[scene_heading] EXT. OLD IRONWORKS - DAY

[action] Will bikes up to the edge of the hulking old structure.  He 
[action] clocks the river flowing just beyond.

[action] Hundreds of black ravens, the same birds from Neibolt, watch 
[action] him from perches all along the skeletal structure.

[scene_heading] EXT. BEVERLY’S HOUSE - DAY

[action] Will sees Bev’s mom leave for work, then bikes around back 
[action] where he clocks a tangle of pipes protruding from the back of 
[action] the building down into a MORLOCK HOLE marked Derry Sewer.

[action] Mr. Marsh watches Bill from the window above.

[scene_heading] EXT. BARRENS - DAY

[action] Will tramps down to the spot where he found the shoe. He 
[action] looks back down the stream, water trickling out of the sewer 
[action] pipe into the Barrens.  The water. 

[action] Something watches him from inside the cylinder of the pipe.

[scene_heading] EXT. NEIBOLT STREET - DAY

[action] Will bikes up to the mouth of the pier, the house suspended 
[action] over the canal, its imploded facade seeming to gape back at 
[action] Will like Munch’s “The Scream.”  Water again.  In Will’s 
[action] mind, it all comes together.
[page_number] 98.

[scene_heading] EXT. EDDIE KASPBRAK’S HOUSE - DAY

[action] Eddie and his mom sit on the couch and watch soaps. Eddie 
[action] plays with a loose thread of his cast, twisting it in his 
[action] fingers, not a friend’s signature anywhere. 

[subheading] KRSSSST

[character] WILL (O.S.)
[dialogue] Eddie? Eddie you there?

[action] His walkie crackles from a high shelf over the TV. Mrs. 
[action] Kaspbrack looks at her son, eyebrows raised.

[character] MRS. KASPBRAK
[dialogue] I thought I took the batteries out 
[dialogue] of that thing?

[action] Eddie shakes his head, as if to say he didn’t know.  She 
[action] points for him to get it.

[character] WILL (O.S.)
[dialogue] Eddie? I know where to find--

[action] He grabs the walkie, holds it tenderly in his hands.

[character] MRS. KASPBRAK
[dialogue] Turn it off.

[action] He complies.  BANG BANG BANG.  They both look at the door.

[scene_heading] INT. EDDIE KASPBRAK’S HOUSE, FRONT DOOR

[action] Mrs. Kaspbrak angrily swings open the door.  A surprised DR. 
[action] HANDOR, Eddie’s general practitioner, is on the porch there.

[character] MRS. KASPBRAK
[dialogue] Oh!  Dr. Handor.  You gave me a 
[dialogue] fright.

[action] She spots Will over his shoulder, straddling his bike in the 
[action] middle of the street.

[character] MRS. KASPBRAK (CONT’D)
[dialogue] C’mon in.

[action] They step inside.  Will pockets his Walkie and goes.

[scene_heading] INT. EDDIE KASPBRAK’S HOUSE, EDDIE’S BEDROOM - DAY

[action] Dr. Handor checks Eddie’s cast. 
[page_number] 99.

[character] DR. HANDOR
[dialogue] How you feeling, son? Any pain?

[character] EDDIE
[dialogue] No.  Feels fine I guess.

[character] DR. HANDOR
[dialogue] Kept the cast pretty clean, I see.  
[dialogue] Didn’t you want any of your friends 
[dialogue] to sign it?

[character] EDDIE
[dialogue] I’m not allowed to see them.

[character] DR. HANDOR
[dialogue] Why not?

[character] EDDIE
[dialogue] My Mom thinks they’re the reason I 
[dialogue] got hurt.  She says I’m sensitive 
[dialogue] and they don’t take care of me like 
[dialogue] she does.

[action] Dr. Handor wants to say something, but hesitates.

[character] DR. HANDOR
[dialogue] She means your allergies?  Is that 
[dialogue] it?

[character] EDDIE
[dialogue] Yeah.  Mom says I should always 
[dialogue] carry my Epi-pen with me, in case I 
[dialogue] ever have an episode.

[character] DR. HANDOR
[dialogue] Mother knows best. Is that it?

[action] Eddie shrugs.

[action] Dr. Handor smiles, considering his next thought. He leans in, 
[action] too close for comfort for Eddie.  For a moment it’s creepy.  

[character] DR. HANDOR (CONT’D)
[dialogue] Eddie, do you know what a placebo 
[dialogue] is?

[scene_heading] EXT. TRAVIS BOWER’S BACKYARD - DAY

[action] A bottle of Southern Comfort sits on a log. BLAM! 

[action] Bowers shoots at it with his dad’s police issue .45. Victor 
[action] and Snatch look on, giggling.
[page_number] 100.

[action] He finally tags it, the bottle shattering into a thousand 
[action] pieces.  They all whoop and holler.

[character] SNATCH
[dialogue] Can I try?

[action] He reaches for the gun.  Bowers pushes him back.

[character] TRAVIS
[dialogue] Sure.  Go put the next target out 
[dialogue] there.

[action] Travis points to the cat that’s lounging there in the grass.

[character] SNATCH
[dialogue] You’re foolin’ right?

[character] TRAVIS
[dialogue] Do I look like I’m foolin’ numb 
[dialogue] nuts?

[action] Snatch grabs the kitty and runs out to the stump. He wipes 
[action] away the glass and sets the cat down. It starts to meow and 
[action] tries to get away.

[character] SNATCH
[dialogue] It’s not staying.

[action] Bower lines up a shot, sight of the barrel on the cat.

[character] TRAVIS
[dialogue] Hold it there, butt cheese.

[character] OFFICER BOWERS (O.C.)
[dialogue] What the hell is this?

[action] Officer Bowers steps from his police cruiser in the driveway. 
[action] His face as red as fire.

[character] TRAVIS
[dialogue] Nothing, pa.  Just cleaning your 
[dialogue] gun like you asked.

[character] OFFICER BOWERS
[dialogue] Cleaning my gun, huh?

[action] He stalks up and grabs the gun from Travis. Smells it. 

[action] WHAP! He smacks Travis hard in the face, knocking the big boy 
[action] to the grass.

[character] OFFICER BOWERS (CONT’D)
[dialogue] Normally I use a rod and cleaner 
[dialogue] not bullets and whiskey.  
[page_number] 101.

[] (MORE)

[character] OFFICER BOWERS (CONT’D) (CONT'D)
[dialogue] You two punks put your sticky 
[dialogue] little fingers on my firearm here?

[action] Victor and Snatch are too scared to talk.  

[character] VICTOR
[dialogue] N-N-No sir.

[action] Butch turns the gun on Travis at his feet.

[character] OFFICER BOWERS
[dialogue] You’re not worth the salt you lick 
[dialogue] boy.  I don’t know how I let you 
[dialogue] squirm your way out your momma’s 
[dialogue] womb.

[character] TRAVIS
[dialogue] Pa, I’m---

[action] BLAM! 

[action] He shoots the ground around Travis feet. Travis squeals like 
[action] a girl, utterly terrified.  BLAM!  BLAM!  A large wet stain 
[action] spreads around his crotch.  Butch Bowers raises the pistol, 
[action] as if he were about to whip Travis, who falls to the ground.

[action] Butch shakes his head.

[character] OFFICER BOWERS
[parenthetical] (to the boys)
[dialogue] Look at him now boys.  Nothing like 
[dialogue] a little fear to make a paper man 
[dialogue] crumble.
[parenthetical] (to Travis)
[dialogue] Clean your drawers ‘fore you come 
[dialogue] inside.

[action] He spits out his disgust, stalks inside.

[action] Victor and Snatch tip-toe over to Travis, not knowing what to 
[action] say or do.  Travis does everything he can not to cry.

[character] VICTOR
[dialogue] You okay, Travis?

[action] An odd wind stirs through the grass there, a yellow menacing 
[action] gathering of clouds overhead.  Travis suddenly notices... 

[action] A BALLOON, bobbing over his mailbox, string connected to 
[action] something stuffed inside.

[action] Travis pushes past his two cronies and goes over to the 
[action] mailbox. Inside he pulls out THE KNIFE.  The one he lost at 
[action] the beginning. And attached to the string a note:
[page_number] 102.

[character] OFFICER BOWERS (CONT’D)

[action]                 TO:    MR. TRAVIS BOWERS
[action]                 FROM:  MR. ROBERT GRAY

[action] A murderous look screws onto Travis’ face as he turns his 
[action] attention to his house.  And his pa inside. 

[action] He snaps the string and starts toward the front door with the 
[action] knife.  The balloon floats up into the darkening sky.

[scene_heading] INT. CORONER’S OFFICE - DAY

[action] A THUNDERCLAP outside. The coroner steps out the door. Will

[dialogue] there.

[dialogue] there’s always water around.  The 
[dialogue] storm drain where Georgie died.

[scene_heading] INT. BEVERLY’S APARTMENT - DAY

[action] Beverly, all gussied up in a pretty white dress, heads for 
[action] the door. A hand snatches out from behind and seizes her arm.

[character] MR. MARSH

[character] BEVERLY

[character] MR. MARSH
[dialogue] You’re all prettied up. Who you all 
[dialogue] prettied up for?

[character] BEVERLY
[dialogue] No one.

[action] He shoves her against the wall, eyes her little body up and 
[action] down.  Caresses her hair.

[character] MR. MARSH
[dialogue] Don’t lie to me, Bevvie.  You know 
[dialogue] how I worry about you.  I worry a 
[dialogue] lot.

[character] BEVERLY
[dialogue] I know daddy.

[character] MR. MARSH
[dialogue] People in town seen you.  Got to 
[dialogue] sayin’ things. Like how my little 
[dialogue] girl been sneakin’ around all 
[dialogue] summer long with a pack of boys.  
[dialogue] Only girl to the pack. 
[page_number] 104.

[character] BEVERLY
[dialogue] They’re my friends, daddy.

[action] He squeezes her cheeks with one hand, holding her head in 
[action] place.

[character] MR. MARSH

[character] BEVERLY
[dialogue] I-- I swear.  They’re just friends.

[character] MR. MARSH
[dialogue] Friends?  I know what’s in them 
[dialogue] boys minds when they look at you, 
[dialogue] Bevvie.  I know all too well.  They 
[dialogue] want to do bad things.  Did you let 
[dialogue] them do bad things to you?

[action] He looms over her. Something in his manner is detached. Like 
[action] he’s there but isn’t there.

[character] BEVERLY
[dialogue] I’m a good girl daddy. Good, just 
[dialogue] like you raised me.

[character] MR. MARSH
[dialogue] Is that so? Then what’s this?

[action] He pulls out Ben’s Haiku Poem.

[character] BEVERLY
[dialogue] Where’d you--

[character] MR. MARSH
[dialogue] You had it squirreled away in your 
[dialogue] underwear drawer.  Why would you 
[dialogue] want to hide it with your underwear 
[dialogue] Bevvie?  What else are you holdin’ 
[dialogue] out from me.

[character] BEVERLY
[dialogue] It’s nothing, daddy.  Just a poem.

[character] MR. MARSH
[parenthetical] (reciting)
[dialogue] Your hair is winter fire,
[dialogue] January embers
[dialogue] My heart burns there, too

[action] He grabs her by the hair and pulls her up to him.
[page_number] 105.

[character] MR. MARSH (CONT’D)
[dialogue] Sounds lusty to me.  Who wrote it.  
[dialogue] Which one of your boyfriends?

[character] BEVERLY
[dialogue] I told you I don’t have one.

[character] MR. MARSH
[dialogue] Don’t lie to me!

[action] He drags her down the hall by the hair.

[character] BEVERLY
[dialogue] Daddy stop. You’re hurting me.

[character] MR. MARSH
[dialogue] You’re ma says you’re a woman now.  
[dialogue] You bled.  What other womanly 
[dialogue] things you been doing down in the 
[dialogue] deep dark woods with those boys, 
[dialogue] Bevvie. 

[action] He throws her into his bedroom.

[character] BEVERLY
[dialogue] Nothing. Please, daddy. You don’t 
[dialogue] have to worry. I promise. Please!

[character] MR. MARSH
[dialogue] Let’s see then. Prove to me you 
[dialogue] been good. 

[action] She doesn’t know what he means.

[character] MR. MARSH (CONT’D)
[dialogue] Take off your tights.

[character] BEVERLY
[dialogue] What?

[character] MR. MARSH
[dialogue] I love you Bevvie. You’re special. 
[dialogue] You’re such a pretty girl. But 
[dialogue] there’s plenty of boys happy to 
[dialogue] roon a pretty girl. Plenty of 
[dialogue] pretty girls willing to be roont. I 
[dialogue] need to know if you’re intact.

[action] He unbuckles his belt, but he doesn’t look at her like he 
[action] wants to whip her, there’s another hunger in his eyes.

[character] MR. MARSH (CONT’D)
[dialogue] Your tights, girl. Take ‘em off. 
[page_number] 106.

[character] BEVERLY
[dialogue] Daddy, please.

[action] He slides off his belt and grabs her, smiling, a smile that 
[action] feels all too reminiscent of a clown.

[character] MR. MARSH
[dialogue] You’ll like it, Bevvie.  Be like 
[dialogue] you’re floating.

[action] Bev kicks him in the balls. Her father lets go, crumpling 
[action] down. She knees him in the face as hard as she can, his head 
[action] flying back into the side of the door.

[action] Beverly tries to race out the door but he snatches her ankle 
[action] and she trips into the hallway. By the time she is up he is 
[action] lunging towards her. 

[action] She stumbles back into the bathroom and shuts the door, 
[action] trying to lock herself inside. He kicks the door open before 
[action] the lock catches. 

[action] Without thinking Bev grabs the toilet top and smashes it in 
[action] her dad’s face. He slumps down, unconscious. 

[action] She rushes out, then uses a chair propped against the knob to 
[action] shut him in the bathroom. Terrified she races out the front 
[action] door.

[scene_heading] INT. EDDIE KASPBRAK’S HOUSE - DAY

[action] Eddie stands in front of his mom, eyes blazing.

[character] MRS. KASPBRAK
[dialogue] Placebo? Who told you that? Dr. 
[dialogue] Handor? He has no right. No right 
[dialogue] to say you’re making up your 
[dialogue] sickness.

[character] EDDIE
[dialogue] Not me, momma... You.

[character] MRS. KASPBRAK
[dialogue] What?

[character] EDDIE
[dialogue] He said I was never allergic until 
[dialogue] you made me start carrying around 
[dialogue] my Epi-pen. You put those allergies 
[dialogue] in my head.
[page_number] 107.

[character] MRS. KASPBRAK
[dialogue] That’s a lie, Eddie!  A bold faced 
[dialogue] lie. You’re my treasure.  The only 
[dialogue] thing I want in this world is to 
[dialogue] see you safe and healthy.

[character] EDDIE
[dialogue] Then why did you send my friends 
[dialogue] away?

[action] She starts to feel the fluttery bird of panic.

[character] MRS. KASPBRAK
[dialogue] They were bad boys.  You know that.  
[dialogue] And you... You were so sick when 
[dialogue] you were little. So very sick.  It 
[dialogue] hurt me so much.  I never want to 
[dialogue] see you go through that again.  And 
[dialogue] I’ve kept you healthy, haven’t I?

[character] EDDIE
[dialogue] You lied to me mamma.  You’ve been 
[dialogue] giving me medicine that’s not 
[dialogue] really medicine. 

[character] MRS. KASPBRAK
[dialogue] Eddie-bear, please--

[character] EDDIE
[dialogue] This is what’s going to happen now.

[action] He pulls out his Epi-pen and throws it in the trash.  Mrs. 
[action] Kaspbrak gasps.

[character] EDDIE (CONT’D)
[dialogue] I’m going out that door and you 
[dialogue] won’t stop me. I’m going to my 
[dialogue] friend Stan’s bar-mitzvah and when 
[dialogue] I come home you’re never going to 
[dialogue] tell how weak or sick or delicate I 
[dialogue] am ever again.

[action] THUNDER crackles outside.

[scene_heading] EXT. DERRY STREETS - DAY

[action] Will and Mike tear through the howling rain on their bikes. 
[action] They almost collide into Beverly who comes dashing around a 
[action] corner.

[character] WILL
[dialogue] Bev. You okay?
[page_number] 108.

[character] BEVERLY
[dialogue] No. No I’m not.

[action] She looks back over her shoulder, terrified and wet and 
[action] shivering.

[character] MIKE
[dialogue] What happened.  Bowers?

[character] BEVERLY
[dialogue] No.  My daddy.  He... he...

[character] WILL
[dialogue] What?  What did he do Beverly?

[action] She looks at both of them, tears running down her face, her 
[action] pretty dress soaked in the rain.

[character] BEVERLY
[dialogue] I don’t know.  I don’t know if it 
[dialogue] was him, or...

[character] MIKE
[dialogue] What?

[character] BEVERLY
[dialogue] I think “IT” was there, controlling 
[dialogue] him, I don’t know, I can’t go back, 
[dialogue] I can’t go back, Will. I don’t know 
[dialogue] what-

[action] She starts to cry on Will’s shoulder.  Will looks at Mike.  
[action] They both look up at the convulsive yellow sky.  SOMETHING 
[action] BIG is about to happen and they both know it.

[scene_heading] INT. SYNAGOGUE - DAY

[action] With torrential downpour outside, leaking water drips from 
[action] the ceiling onto the crowd below, who have cheerfully 
[action] employed umbrellas indoor to deal with the nuisance.

[action] Stan is giving a nervous, but otherwise successful reading of 
[action] the Torah, despite the howling wind and end of days vibe.

[action] Richie and Ben sit in the back in their little jackets.

[character] BEN
[dialogue] This storm is crazy.  Like the 
[dialogue] sky’s gonna fall. 

[action] SLAM!  Everyone looks back towards the door, blown open by 
[action] the wind, and at Eddie, who sheepishly was trying to slip in. 
[page_number] 109.

[action] Stan smiles at his friend, pleased he’s out of “mom jail.”  
[action] The CANTOR gives him a nudge and continues reading.

[action] Eddie slides in meekly next to Richie and Ben, checking his 
[action] clip-on bowtie.

[character] RICHIE
[parenthetical] (through his teeth)
[dialogue] Way to make an entry, Eddie-bear.

[character] BEN
[dialogue] How’d you escape?

[character] EDDIE
[dialogue] Guess I’m not as weak as I thought.

[scene_heading] INT. TRAVIS BOWER’S HOUSE - DAY

[action] The front door creaks open, Victor and Snatch poking their 
[action] heads in, both soaked to the bone.

[character] SNATCH
[dialogue] Travis?

[character] VICTOR
[dialogue] Mr. Bowers?

[action] Nothing. They see a streak of blood on the floor that leads 
[action] to the KITCHEN and Butch Bowers on the linoleum floor in a 
[action] red pool, stabbed dozens of times, the knife left in his eye. 

[character] SNATCH
[dialogue] Holy shit.

[action] Victor vomits.  The cat slips in and starts licking up the 
[action] blood.

[character] SNATCH (CONT’D)
[dialogue] Travis, dude, you okay?

[action] Snatch continues on into the LIVING ROOM where he finds 
[action] TRAVIS sitting in Butch’s favorite Lazy-boy in front of the 
[action] TV, which is turned on to PENNYWISE THE CLOWN SHOW.  

[action] He holds the .45, All slicked in blood, his eyes glazed.

[character] TRAVIS
[dialogue] It’s my gun now.

[action] He turns and looks at Snatch and Victor in the doorway. 
[page_number] 110.

[scene_heading] INT. SYNAGOGUE - DAY

[action] The STORM GROWLS outside.  Richie, Ben and Eddie look at each 
[action] other, a little on edge.  Suddenly the doors crash open again 
[action] and Stan stops, once again, looking up...

[action] Will, Bev, and Mike slink into the back of the synagogue, 
[action] soaking wet.  The Rabbi’s wife shoots them a look of daggers.

[scene_heading] INT. SYNAGOGUE - DAY

[action] The reading is over and the crowd starts to gather to leave 
[action] for the reception.  Will, Bev, Ben, Richie, Eddie, and Mike 
[action] are all huddled together.  Stan is the only one not there.

[character] RICHIE
[dialogue] What do you mean your dad attacked 
[dialogue] you?

[character] BEVERLY
[dialogue] It was like that lumberjack Ben 
[dialogue] told us about.  The one from the 
[dialogue] Silver Dollar.  He had this look. 
[dialogue] Like someone was pushing him to do 
[dialogue] it.

[character] WILL
[dialogue] Not someone. “IT”.

[action] Eddie, thinking about his mother.

[character] EDDIE
[dialogue] You guys, if “IT” can take over 
[dialogue] Beverly’s parents, why not the rest 
[dialogue] of ours?

[character] BEVERLY
[dialogue] I can’t go back there guys. He’ll 
[dialogue] kill me. Or worse.

[action] This sinks in to everyone. There’s only one thing they can 
[action] do.

[dialogue] Will knows where to find “IT.”

[character] EDDIE
[dialogue] What do you mean?

[character] WILL
[dialogue] The sewers.  Me and Mike figured it 
[dialogue] out.  That’s how it gets around. 
[dialogue] That’s where it lives.  
[page_number] 111.

[character] RICHIE
[dialogue] Whoa whoa whoa.  You don’t really 
[dialogue] mean--

[character] BEN 
[dialogue] If it means protecting Beverly, 
[dialogue] count me in. 

[action] Beverly looks at Ben, consistently impressed by his defense 
[action] of her.

[character] EDDIE

[character] WILL
[dialogue] The sewers. We go in. All of us.

[character] RICHIE
[dialogue] And friggin do what?  In this 
[dialogue] storm?

[action] Water is actively dripping on them.

[character] WILL
[dialogue] Get rid of “IT” before “IT” gets 
[dialogue] rid of us, one by one.  It doesn’t 
[dialogue] know what to do when all of us are 
[dialogue] together, you have to believe me.  

[scene_heading] INT./EXT. SYNAGOGUE - DAY

[action] Stan shakes hands with his aunts and uncles near the front 
[action] door.  The wind whips up the women’s dresses and unhinges 
[action] umbrellas.  Will and the gang run to him and whisk him out.

[character] STAN
[dialogue] Hey guys.  How’d I do?

[character] RICHIE
[dialogue] I hope your schlong grew those 12 
[dialogue] inches cuz you’re gonna need it.

[character] STAN
[dialogue] What do you mean?

[character] WILL
[dialogue] C’mon!

[action] The storm picks up force, wind blowing umbrellas inside out 
[action] and from the hands of guests trudging back to the parking 
[action] lot. One goes tumbling across the gardens and into...

[action] THE BLACK BOILING RIVER and sinks into the dark water.
[page_number] 112.

[scene_heading] INT. ZACH DENBROUGH’S WORKSHOP - DAY

[action] Will and the gang arrive like their own hurricane in the dark 
[action] cellar workshop.  Whipping around the room and picking up 
[action] objects, Will unfurls a map on his dad’s work table. 

[action] The other kids stock up on whatever armor or weapons they can 
[action] find -- crossbow, hockey pads, a hubcap as shield, a 
[action] sledgehammer, a hockey stick shaved into a stake, satchel 
[action] full of car flares, hairspray. Stan is still processing 
[action] what’s going on.  Mixed with their formal wear for the event, 
[action] they look mildly ridiculous.

[character] STAN
[dialogue] Hold on. How are we-?

[action] Richie hands Stan a chainsaw.

[character] RICHIE
[dialogue] Happy bar mitzvah, Jason.  Bet you 
[dialogue] didn’t put this sucker on your gift 
[dialogue] list.

[action] Someone pounds at the door.

[character] ZACH (O.S.)
[dialogue] Will, what are you doing in there? 
[dialogue] Open up.

[action] Everyone looks to Will.  He folds up the map into a backpack.

[character] WILL
[dialogue] We need to protect each other Stan.  
[dialogue] The seven of us is all we have.  
[dialogue] Together “IT” can’t hurt us.  I 
[dialogue] promise.

[character] STAN
[dialogue] How do you know?

[action] More knocking.

[character] ZACH (O.S.)
[dialogue] Willy, now!  We need to talk.

[action] Will steps up to Stan.

[character] WILL
[dialogue] I have faith.  Just like you.

[action] Stan looks to the others, everyone scared but ready for 
[action] battle.  Stans steels himself, owning up his chainsaw.  The 
[action] door shakes with Zach behind it.
[page_number] 113.

[character] ZACH
[dialogue] Damn it, Will!

[action] Will opens the back window and the kids slip out.

[scene_heading] EXT. OLD IRONWORKS - DAY

[action] The losers -- ALL 7 OF THEM -- stand on the threshold of the 
[action] Old Ironworks cellar hold, the very same one PATRICK 
[action] HOCKSTETTLER died in... a yawning chasm of mud, pools of 
[action] water and hulking, shattered old boilers.  

[action] The rain and wind howls, lightning cracking overhead.  Will 
[action] points to a huge SEWER PIPE that juts out of the muck.

[character] WILL
[parenthetical] (yelling)
[dialogue] This is our only way into Derry’s 
[dialogue] original sewer system before it was 
[dialogue] sealed off and modernized.

[action] BLING!

[action] A bullet sparks off a iron post next to Will’s head.

[action] Travis, Snatch and Victor stand at the crest of the road 
[action] leading to the factory.  Travis has his dad’s gun, aimed like 
[action] Dirty Harry at them.  He pops off another round, BLANG-PING.

[character] BEN
[dialogue] Oh, fuck!

[action] Will grabs Bev’s hand, who grabs Ben’s who grab’s Eddie’s and 
[action] on down the line.  They follow Will around the cellar hold to 
[action] a giant conveyor belt toppled against the edge. They scale 
[action] down it into the chasm, bullets pinging around them.  

[action] CLICK CLICK CLICK.  Travis runs out of ammo.  He throws the 
[action] gun away and pulls out the bloody knife.

[character] VICTOR
[dialogue] Man, this is where Patrick 
[dialogue] Hockstettler went missing.

[character] SNATCH
[dialogue] No way I’m going down there.

[action] Travis glares at Snatch, PENNYWISE appearing near the boy in 
[action] a flash of lightning. 

[action] Travis lunges with the knife and plunges it into Snatch’s 
[action] tummy.  Victor watches in horror.
[page_number] 114.

[character] TRAVIS
[parenthetical] (to Victor)
[dialogue] Still have second thoughts?

[action] Victor shakes his head no.  Snatch stumbles back and plunges 
[action] into the darkness of the cellar hold...

[scene_heading] INT. CELLAR HOLD - SAME

[action] SMACK!  Snatch’s lifeless body slams into the muck right in 
[action] front of Will and the others.

[character] EDDIE
[dialogue] Oh my God oh my God!

[character] WILL
[dialogue] Don’t look back!

[action] They dart into the blackness of the huge, rusty pipe.  

[action] Travis turns back to Victor, a dull craze in his eyes.  He 
[action] gestures to the conveyor the kids climbed down.

[character] TRAVIS
[dialogue] After you.

[scene_heading] INT. SEWER TUNNELS - DAY

[action] The losers follow Will, who uses his dad's map to lead them 
[action] deeper and deeper into the sewers which roar and chime with 
[action] rushing water. They turn a corner and come to...

[subheading] A DEAD END

[action] The stream of water seems to disappear under the stone wall 
[action] there.  Everyone is terrified.

[character] BEVERLY
[dialogue] Now what?

[action] He shines his light on something in the corner...  

[character] WILL

[dialogue] here.  The last tunnel that’s shown 
[dialogue] on the map.

[centered] STOP NOW OR I’LL KILL YOU ALL!

[character] BEN

[character] WILL

[action] Stan finds a loose stone.

[character] STAN
[dialogue] Here.  Will, over here.

[action] They all kick at the stones, which give way to...

[scene_heading] INT. SEWER TUNNELS - DAY

[action] Travis and Victor race down the tunnels after them.  They 
[action] turn the corner and come to the dead end, a pile of stones 
[action] kicked out from a BLACK HOLE.  Travis kneels down and looks 
[action] into it. He can see Will and the gang’s FLASHLIGHTS...

[character] TRAVIS
[dialogue] I seeeeeeeeeee yooooooouuuu losers!

[scene_heading] INT. BLACK HOLE - DAY

[action] Will and the others slosh on their hands and knees down the 
[action] hole, with roots and bursting through its stone ribs. 

[action] It grows narrower and narrower to the point of complete and 
[action] utter claustrophobia. Mike looks back past Ben and sees... 

[action] Two shadowy figures crawling toward them.
[page_number] 116.

[character] MIKE
[dialogue] Hurry!

[action] Will, leading them, comes to a SMALL CORKSCREW in a tangle of 
[action] roots blocking them they all have to wrench their way around. 

[action] Ben, the last of the group, GETS STUCK.

[character] BEN
[dialogue] No no no no no!  Guys!

[action] Travis bears down, his knife clenched in his teeth. 

[character] TRAVIS
[dialogue] I’m gonna carve you up, tits.  I’m 
[dialogue] going to stick this blade right up 
[dialogue] your asshole and split you in two.  
[dialogue] Feed your ass fat to the friggin’ 
[dialogue] pigs.

[action] He starts squealing like a pig.  Ben is freaked.  Two pair of 
[action] hands grab Ben’s arms and yank hard.  He wrenches through the 
[action] corkscrew and stumbles out into...

[scene_heading] INT. MEMBRANOUS CHAMBER - DAY

[action] A thin membrane floats over gelatinous water, very delicate, 
[action] almost skin like. Underneath, thousands of spiders swim, nest 
[action] and writhe.  He can actually feel their legs push against the 
[action] underside of the layer.

[character] BEN
[dialogue] Oh my God oh my God oh my God.

[character] WILL
[dialogue] Don’t jiggle it too much, whatever 
[dialogue] you do.

[character] MIKE
[dialogue] We don’t want it to tear.

[action] Ben freezes, the spiders knowing he’s there and gathering 
[action] under his butt.  Bev, Stan, Eddie and Mike look on from solid 
[action] ground on the other side of the membrane.

[character] BEVERLY
[dialogue] Take off your shoes.  You’ll be 
[dialogue] fine.  Just move real real slow.

[action] Ben, Mike and Will take off their sneaks and crawl across the 
[action] membrane with great care.  SPIDERS GATHER wherever they press 
[action] a knee or hand, the whole thing sloshing like a water bed. 
[page_number] 117.

[scene_heading] INT. BLACK HOLE - DAY

[action] Travis and Victor get to the corkscrew pass.  There’s no way 
[action] Travis, huge compared to the other kids, will fit through.

[character] TRAVIS
[dialogue] No! No no no no! Fuck!

[character] VICTOR
[dialogue] We’ll never fit.  We’ve gotta go 
[dialogue] back. 

[action] Travis grabs Victor by the scruff of the neck.  A crooked, 
[action] bloodthirsty look flashes across his ugly face.

[character] TRAVIS
[dialogue] I need to hear them squeal.  Now 
[dialogue] you’ll fit.

[action] He puts the knife into Victor’s hand and points to keep 
[action] going.  For a split second Victor thinks about stabbing him, 
[action] but he’s too scared.  He squeezes his rangy frame through the 
[action] roots into...

[scene_heading] INT. MEMBRANOUS CHAMBER - DAY

[action] Victor flops out onto the membrane just as Ben, Will and Mike 
[action] join the others on firm ground.

[character] EDDIE
[dialogue] Hurry.

[action] Victor sees them.  He’s freaked.

[character] VICTOR
[dialogue] No.  Guys.  Wait!  Please!  Travis 
[dialogue] has gone insane.  I’m not trying to 
[dialogue] hurt you, just... 

[action] Now noticing the room he is in, realizing the danger.

[character] VICTOR (CONT’D)
[dialogue] Take me with you!

[action] Everyone looks to Will, who sees something on the membrane 
[action] that gives him pause.

[character] WILL
[dialogue] Victor.

[action] Will is pointing.  Beverly follows his eyes to the knife in 
[action] Victor’s hand, which has PUNCTURED THE MEMBRANE. 
[page_number] 118.

[character] BEVERLY
[dialogue] Oh no-

[action] Spiders spill out onto it’s surface.

[character] VICTOR
[dialogue] Oh god oh god!

[action] He freaks, spiders jumping on him, his motion only ripping 
[action] the membrane more.  It finally tears and Victor plunges 
[action] beneath the membrane, spiders devouring him, Victor screaming 
[action] in agony as they fill his mouth.

[action] Horrified, Will and the others run for their lives.

[scene_heading] INT. BLACK HOLE - DAY

[action] Travis hears the screaming, enjoying it.

[character] TRAVIS
[dialogue] Good.  That’s good, Vic.  Kill ‘em.  
[dialogue] Kill ‘em all! 

[action] Suddenly spiders start spilling out from behind the roots of 
[action] the corkscrew.  Travis screams, and kicks them away.  But 
[action] more come.  Freaked, he retreats back to the sewer tunnels.

[scene_heading] INT. IT’S CAVERN - DAY

[action] Will, Ben, Bev, Mike, Eddie, Stan and Richie stumble from the 
[action] dark into a massive, soaring, subterranean Pantheon-like 
[action] hall, sewer water streaming past their feet...

[action] In the center of the floor is a massive OCULUS, open to what 
[action] appears to be the edge of INFINITE SPACE.  The water from the 
[action] sewers flows down into seven channels around this opening, 
[action] ending at...

[action] SEVEN WATERFALLS THAT FALL UPWARDS into a reflecting pool 
[action] that covers much of the ceiling, surrounding a stone island 
[action] with a CRYPT TYPE STRUCTURE.

[action] The whole cavern seems to be lit from within, a sickly, 
[action] pulsing ambient light coming from everywhere and nowhere.

[character] RICHIE
[dialogue] Something tells me we’re not in 
[dialogue] Derry anymore.

[character] BEN
[dialogue] You think?
[page_number] 119.

[action] Will heads toward the edge of the oculus, where the 
[action] waterfalls stream up.

[character] EDDIE
[dialogue] Don’t get too close to the edge, 
[dialogue] Will.

[character] MIKE
[dialogue] It’s up there isn’t it?  That’s 
[dialogue] where “IT” sleeps.

[action] Pointing at the reflecting pool.

[character] STAN
[dialogue] You mean Pennywise.

[character] WILL
[dialogue] It doesn’t matter what it calls 
[dialogue] itself.  What matters is it’s 
[dialogue] afraid to show us what it really 
[dialogue] is.  Which means we can beat it.

[action] Will sees Beverly over by one of the waterfalls.

[character] BEVERLY
[dialogue] Guess I’m going first again?

[character] WILL
[dialogue] Beverly, wait-!

[action] She jumps into the waterfall and it sweeps her up up up to 
[action] the ceiling.

[action] Will and the boys swap holy shit looks.  Ben jumps in after 
[action] her, then Will, Mike, and Stan.

[action] Richie looks to Eddie, who has the same terrified look as at 
[action] the quarry.  He holds out his hand.

[character] RICHIE
[dialogue] You still afraid of a little grey 
[dialogue] water?

[character] EDDIE
[dialogue] You still wear diapers at night?

[action] Eddie grabs Richie’s hand and they jump into the waterfall 
[action] together. They are swept up up up into...
[page_number] 120.

[scene_heading] INT. THE REFLECTING POOL ON THE CEILING - CONTINUOUS

[action] Plunging into the pool with the others.  For a moment they 
[action] all flap and kick under water, unsure which way is up, then 
[action] Bev grabs them both and pulls them to the surface.

[action] They’re upside down now, the oculus opening to space below 
[action] them, gravity seeming to work by its own rules in here..

[character] WILL
[dialogue] We all here?

[action] Richie looks back where they just “fell” from.

[character] RICHIE
[dialogue] Mind equals blown right now.

[action] The camera flips around and THE WORLD RIGHTS ITSELF, the 
[action] oculus now above.  The pool, which seemed small before, is 
[action] now enormous -- like a dark, brooding lake -- the crypt 
[action] island very far from them. 

[character] MIKE
[dialogue] This is nothing like what it looked 
[dialogue] like from below.

[character] EDDIE
[dialogue] It’s in our head.  All the stuff we 
[dialogue] see.  It’s like the allergies my 
[dialogue] mom said I had.  We make it real.

[character] STAN
[dialogue] We do.  Or IT?

[character] WILL
[dialogue] Guys, the island.  Let’s swim.

[action] They splash toward the crypt island.  Ben is the first to 
[action] feel something brush past his leg.  A replay of the quarry.

[character] BEN
[dialogue] Not funny, Richie.

[action] He looks back and sees no one.  Richie is a couple of lengths 
[action] to his left.

[character] RICHIE
[dialogue] What?

[action] Ben suddenly realizes.  Bev also feels something on her foot.

[character] BEVERLY
[dialogue] The water.  There’s something in 
[dialogue] the water with us.
[page_number] 121.

[action] Suddenly a WORMY TENTACLE extends from the water behind Mike.  
[action] Will sees it and whacks it away with his baseball bat.

[character] WILL
[dialogue] Hurry!

[action] The kids hustle it freestyle across the pool for the island. 

[action] WE PULL BACK WIDE TO SEE a monstrously big, faintly outlined 
[action] starfish-like creature with an orange glowing eye, lurking in 
[action] the depths of the reflecting pool.

[action] Will, then Bev, then Richie dodge a tentacle and all get to 
[action] the island. Stan is not far behind, with Mike, Eddie and Ben 
[action] taking the rear, swatting back more tentacles.

[action] From under Stan the ORANGE EYE APPEARS, the dark water around 
[action] him suddenly aglow. He looks down into it and it becomes 
[action] clear the eye is really a mouth, and the orange is coming 
[action] from deep inside its bowels -- THE DEADLIGHTS.

[action] Stan looks directly into them and is mesmerized, so much so 
[action] that he stops swimming.

[character] WILL (CONT’D)
[dialogue] Stan what are you doing!

[action] Paralyzed, Stan starts to sink toward the deadlights when 
[action] Mike, shielding his eyes, grabs Stan and swims him back to 
[action] the surface.  

[action] All the kids give cover with their weapons -- shooting 
[action] flares, swinging the sledgehammer, impaling with the stake, 
[action] using the hubcap as a shield -- so that Mike, helped by Ben 
[action] and Eddie, can drag Stan onto the island.

[character (DUAL)] EDDIE
[dialogue (DUAL)] Stan? Stan, are you okay?
[character (DUAL)] BEVERLY
[dialogue (DUAL)] Stan!

[action] He stares blankly ahead, as if his mind was wiped blank.

[character] WILL (CONT’D)
[dialogue] Stan, it’s us.  You in there buddy?

[action] Another tentacle squirms out of the water toward them.  Ben 
[action] shoots it with a crossbow.  It goes slithering back in.

[character] BEN
[dialogue] Guys.  I don’t think this is the 
[dialogue] best place to hang out right now.

[character] EDDIE
[dialogue] Stanley, snap out of it.
[page_number] 122.

[action] Stan blinks conscious.  He looks at them all.

[character] STAN
[dialogue] Where am I?

[character] RICHIE
[dialogue] The worst and only bar mitzvah 
[dialogue] party you will ever have.

[action] They help Stan to his feet. Mike looks back over his shoulder 
[action] for the monster.

[character] MIKE
[dialogue] Guys, the water thing.  IT’s gone.

[action] Indeed, the reflecting pool is placid as ice.  

[action] Will looks back to the crypt, an obsidian stone heptagon-
[action] shaped structure the size of one of Mike’s killing pens.

[character] WILL
[dialogue] Maybe.  Or just changing into 
[dialogue] something else.

[character] EDDIE
[dialogue] What do you mean?

[character] WILL
[dialogue] It’s using our fears against us.  
[dialogue] It wants us to be afraid.

[character] EDDIE
[dialogue] Because we’ll taste better.

[character] RICHIE
[dialogue] And that’s supposed to rally us, 
[dialogue] Will?

[character] WILL
[dialogue] We have to stick together, to help 
[dialogue] each other against that kind of 
[dialogue] fear you only have when you’re 
[dialogue] alone.  Alright?

[action] They all nod and follow Will onto the structure, starry deep 
[action] space through the oculus visible over their shoulders.  They 
[action] come to a child-sized TRAP DOOR with an ancient, cosmic 
[action] marking. The only way in or out. 

[action] WILL PUTS HIS HAND ON THE LATCH

[character] WILL (CONT’D)
[dialogue] Ready?
[page_number] 123.

[action] The kids steal themselves, weapons ready.  Mike hammers a 
[action] clip into the stone and ties a rope to it.  Nods all around.

[subheading] WILL TURNS THE LATCH

[action] The door creaks open.  Nothing but pure blackness inside.  
[action] Mike throws down the rope.  Will looks at Bev.

[character] WILL (CONT’D)
[dialogue] My turn.

[action] Will takes a breath, drops in...

[scene_heading] INT. IT’S CRYPT - DAY

[action] Will shimmies down about 5 feet of rope and lights a car 
[action] flare, illuminating the chamber in a blood red pall. The 
[action] walls inside are rough, like the surface of a meteor.

[action] Directly under the trapdoor is a fountain like hole in the 
[action] floor open to the reflecting pool.  Floating on the water is 

[action] GEORGIE’S PAPER BOAT

[action] Pennywise emerges from the shadows behind Will, clinging to 
[action] the wall, joints all turned and wrong, like a spider.

[character] PENNYWISE
[dialogue] Welcome to the circus, Willy.

[action] SLAM!

[action] The trapdoor slams shut and slices the rope.  Will falls to 
[action] the floor, flare falling into the water.  It dims but doesn’t 
[action] go out.

[action] It’s near PITCH BLACK.  Just Will and Pennywise.  He can hear 
[action] his friends banging on the door, calling for him but can’t 
[action] see anything. 

[action] There’s scuffling all around him.  Close.  Will dives into 
[action] the water for the flare.  Rises. Sweeps it around.  

[action] NOTHING.

[character] WILL
[dialogue] I’m not afraid of you, clown. 

[character] PENNYWISE
[dialogue] Your brother wasn’t afraid either.

[action] A PAIR OF ORANGE EYES GLOW ABOVE HIM, IN THE DARK.  Will 
[action] can’t see him.
[page_number] 124.

[character] PENNYWISE (CONT’D)
[dialogue] Not until I chewed his little 
[dialogue] muscles up.

[action] Will turns just in time for the eyes to recede into the 
[action] black.  The pounding on the trap door has stopped.

[character] WILL
[dialogue] Guys?

[action] PENNYWISE LUNGES FROM THE CEILING

[character] WILL (CONT’D)
[dialogue] Ahhhh!

[action] Will drops his bag of flares and scrambles off, tripping over 
[action] the hole in the floor, leg sloshing in the pool.

[action] Glimpses of Pennywise scuffling in the dark like a spider.

[action] Will finds a corner, the flare running out of spark.  He 
[action] waves it this way and that, illuminating the dark in front of 
[action] him.  Swinging his baseball bat wildly at shadows.

[character] WILL (CONT’D)
[dialogue] Come out from the dark then.  Come 
[dialogue] out from behind that stupid mask 
[dialogue] and show me what you really are. 
[dialogue] C’mon!

[action] Will looks at the last light of his flare, waiting for the 
[action] end.  He looks the bat in his clutch only to see it’s morphed 
[action] into... 

[action] A BOY’S SEVERED ARM

[action] He throws it away into the darkness.  The flare goes out.

[subheading] PITCH BLACK

[character] PENNYWISE
[dialogue] Ooooh I’d love to, Will.  I’d just 
[dialogue] love to show you my deadlights.  
[dialogue] Only you wouldn’t understand.  And 
[dialogue] I need you to, Willy, I really 
[dialogue] really need you to understand 
[dialogue] what’s going to happen to you.  
[dialogue] What it means for a little kiddie 
[dialogue] like you... to float.

[action] A light seems to emanate from the pool underneath them, 
[action] illuminating the space, illuminating...

[action] PENNYWISE, STANDING IN THE CORNER
[page_number] 125.

[action] Will sees all of “IT” now.  From his bare crusty feet to his 
[action] bald head.  His claw-like hands and his emaciated acrobat-
[action] like body.  He floats just a few inches over the stone floor.

[action] Will’s eyes fall to the baseball bat half-way between them.  
[action] He lunges for it, Pennywise knocking it away.  The clown 
[action] seizes his neck, smiles hungrily, bares his twisted fangs.

[character] WILL
[dialogue] I’m sorry, Georgie.

[action] Pennywise goes for a bite and...

[subheading] THWACK

[action] An arrow shoots through it’s face.  The injured clown recoils 
[action] away.  Bev flops out from the reflecting pool, followed by 
[action] Ben, Richie, Stan, Eddie, and Mike.

[character] BEVERLY
[dialogue] Break into our homes, we break into 
[dialogue] yours.

[action] Pennywise pulls the arrow from his bloody mouth.

[character] BEN
[dialogue] Charge!

[action] THE LOSERS ATTACK PENNYWISE WITH EVERYTHING THEY GOT

[action] Mike with crow bar, Stan with chainsaw, Ben with a hub cap 
[action] shield and sledgehammer, Richie with his hockey stick stake, 
[action] Eddie with a nail gun.  Will grabs his bat and joins them.

[action] Pennywise tries to fend off this “Lord of the Flies” 
[action] rebellion, becoming bigger, fiercer, more grotesque, but the 
[action] kids are too much.  Just when they have “IT” bleeding and 
[action] backed into a corner for the death blow...

[character] PENNYWISE
[dialogue] Wait!

[action] Will grabs Ben’s sledgehammer.  The creature that looks up at 
[action] them isn’t Pennywise but... GEORGIE

[character] PENNYWISE/GEORGIE
[dialogue] Please, Willy.  Please don’t hurt 
[dialogue] me.

[character] WILL
[dialogue] You’re not Georgie.
[page_number] 126.

[character] PENNYWISE/GEORGIE
[dialogue] I am.  I’m in here, Willy.  We all 
[dialogue] are.  This is the place we float.

[character] BEVERLY
[dialogue] Will, it’s a trick.

[character] PENNYWISE/GEORGIE
[dialogue] It’s not your fault Willy.  Not 
[dialogue] you’re fault I’m stuck here.  But 
[dialogue] you’re hurting us all.  

[character] MIKE
[dialogue] It’s trying to get to you, Will. 
[dialogue] Kill it. Kill it now.

[character] WILL
[dialogue] No!  I have to think.

[character] PENNYWISE/GEORGIE
[dialogue] Mom and dad they should know.  They 
[dialogue] should know it’s not your fault.  
[dialogue] I’ll tell ‘em.  I’ll tell ‘em as 
[dialogue] long as you don’t hurt us anymore.

[action] Will is confused. This is the one thing he wanted to hear. 
[action] Beverly hands him his satchel with the car flares.

[character] BEVERLY
[dialogue] Finish him, Will.  For all of us. 
[dialogue] For Georgie.

[action] Will looks to the scared little boy on the ground.

[character] RICHIE
[dialogue] I’m taking this fucker out.

[character] WILL
[dialogue] No.

[action] Will grabs his stake away.  He turns to Georgie.  Kneels 
[action] down.  Takes his wet little head in his hands.

[character] WILL (CONT’D)
[dialogue] I’m sorry.  I’m sorry I didn’t go 
[dialogue] out there with you.  I’m sorry I 
[dialogue] was sick.

[character] GEORGIE
[dialogue] It’s okay, Willy.  I know.  
[dialogue] Everything’ll be okay.

[action] ***WILL DOES THE CALL BACK AND SOMETHING IN THE WAY GEORGIE 
[action] RESPONDS SNAPS WILL OUT OF HIS STUPOR.  GEORGIE’S GONE***
[page_number] 127.

[character] WILL
[dialogue] Hairspray.

[action] Bev sprays it through his lit flare.  Torching Georgie.

[character] GEORGIE
[dialogue] No!  No Willy!  Please!

[action] GEORGIE REVERTS BACK TO PENNYWISE, screaming horribly and 
[action] lunging at the kids one last time. IT finally gives up, 
[action] slumping to the floor dead, a black charred clownish corpse.

[action] Richie touches the remains and they deflates, almost as if a 
[action] balloon.  

[action] The trapdoor above them blows away, as if it were sucked off 
[action] by a cyclone, out of the oculus, and into what looks like the 
[action] stars of outer space.

[character] EDDIE
[dialogue] Did we kill it?

[action] Will isn’t sure, he looks at the rest of them.

[character] MIKE
[dialogue] You did it, Will.

[action] Bev hugs him.  Ben watches, enviously.

[character] RICHIE
[dialogue] It’s miller ti--

[action] Suddenly the GRAVITY SPELL WEARS OFF and the kids all fall to 
[action] the ceiling of the stone crypt. Bev almost falls out into 
[action] space, caught by Ben just in time.

[action] The water from the waterfalls and from the reflecting pool 
[action] all collapse back toward the floor, along with some of the 
[action] stones in the crypt around them, which begins to crumble.

[action] At first the losers hang on, afraid to fall, but eventually 
[action] they lose grip and fall toward the oculus, which has begun to 
[action] fill up with the water from the falls and reflecting pool.

[action] They all plunge safely into the new pool below, Will’s foot 
[action] kicking out the bottom of it into outer space before being 
[action] pulled back by Stan.

[character] WILL
[dialogue] We’ve got to get out of here.
[page_number] 128.

[scene_heading] INT. DERRY SEWERS - DAY

[action] The losers hurry their way out of the sewers, storm water 
[action] quickly rising around them.  Will leads the way with a car 
[action] flare.  They come to another dead-end grate.

[character] RICHIE
[dialogue] Shit!

[character] WILL
[dialogue] We’ll get out. C’mon.

[scene_heading] INT. ANOTHER PIPE - LATER

[action] They continue to look for a way out, the water now up to 
[action] their chests. The losers look cold, lost, and in despair.

[character] STAN
[dialogue] We’re going to drown down here 
[dialogue] aren’t we?

[character (DUAL)] EDDIE
[dialogue (DUAL)] I’m so cold.
[character (DUAL)] MIKE
[dialogue (DUAL)] All this water. It’s still 
[dialogue (DUAL)] going to win.

[character] WILL
[dialogue] No. I don’t know--

[character] BEN
[dialogue] We’re dead. So dead.

[character] BEVERLY
[dialogue] Guys, stop it.  Focus.

[action] Everyone turns to Bev. Their muse. Their light.

[action] SHE TAKES EDDIE’S FACE IN HER HANDS

[action] SHE TAKES STAN’S FACE IN HER HANDS

[action] SHE TAKES RICHIE’S FACE IN HER HANDS

[action] SHE TAKES MIKE’S FACE IN HER HANDS

[action] SHE TAKES BEN’S FACE IN HER HANDS

[action] SHE TAKES Will’S FACE IN HER HANDS
[page_number] 129.

[scene_heading] EXT. PENOBSCOT RIVER - DAY

[action] One by one the kids shoot out of a STORM PIPE into the 
[action] PENOBSCOT RIVER, just like Georgie’s paper boat. They pop up 
[action] like tops, blinking owlishly against the daylight. 

[action] The sky is blue.  The storm has passed.

[scene_heading] EXT. RIVERBANK - DUSK

[action] Will, Bev, Mike, Ben, Richie, Eddie and Stan slump soaked, 
[action] shivering, and exhausted on the embankment. Derry is in full 
[action] view across the river, a red ball setting behind it.

[action] They gather themselves on the bank, breathing hard as the 
[action] adrenaline of survival courses through their veins.

[character] RICHIE
[dialogue] Now can we celebrate?

[character] BEVERLY
[dialogue] Will?

[action] Stan sees an OBJECT GLITTER in the sunlight. He picks it up, 
[action] face sober and thoughtful -- an old GLASS COKE BOTTLE. He 
[action] takes it by the neck, shatters it against a rock, and

[action] LOOKS TO WILL, NODDING

[action] Will knows exactly what this means. He steps up to Stan, who 
[action] SLASHES Will’S PALM with the shard. One by one the others 
[action] approach, Stan slashing everyone’s palms, does his own last.

[character] WILL
[dialogue] Swear, guys.  Swear if IT isn’t 
[dialogue] dead, if it ever returns, we’ll 
[dialogue] come back and fight it together.

[action] They all clasp hands and lock eyes. A BLOOD OATH IS SWORN.

[scene_heading] INT. TRAVIS BOWERS HOUSE - DUSK

[action] Travis, hair now SHOCK WHITE, expression blank and broken, 
[action] sits in his chair in front of white fuzz of the TV.  His 
[action] father’s corpse is still on the floor, blood matted 
[action] everywhere.  POUNDING ON THE FRONT DOOR.
[page_number] 130.

[scene_heading] EXT. TRAVIS BOWERS HOUSE - NIGHT

[action] Chief Borton and other Derry cops frog march Travis out of 
[action] the house in cuffs through a scene of POLICE, NEWS REPORTERS, 
[action] and a CORONER CARTING OUT A BODY BAG.  

[scene_heading] INT. HOSPITAL CORRIDOR - NIGHT

[action] Mike races past nurses and doctors, a swell of optimism 
[action] building with every stride. He turns the corner into...

[scene_heading] INT. HOSPITAL ROOM - NIGHT

[action] Leroy’s bed is empty. His mom is packing up his dad’s things. 
[action] She looks up, tears in her eyes. Mike is devastated.

[character] MIKE
[dialogue] But we beat It.  We beat the 
[dialogue] cancer.

[] MIKE’S MOM
[] What are you talking about?

[character] MIKE
[dialogue] Derry.  The clown.  It.

[action] Mike’s mom takes him into her arms.

[] MIKE’S MOM
[] He’s in a better place, Mikey.  I 
[] know it’s hard to believe.  But you 
[] have to.  Remember what your father 
[] always said...

[scene_heading] EXT. HANLON FARM - DAY (FLASHBACK)

[action] Leroy shows Mike a baby lamb, freshly born from its mother’s 
[action] womb.

[character] LEROY
[dialogue] See son, everything has a cycle. 
[dialogue] Just when you think the world 
[dialogue] couldn’t get any colder, something 
[dialogue] good always grows.

[scene_heading] INT. HOSPITAL ROOM - NIGHT

[action] Mike holds his mother tight, remembering, tears rolling down 
[action] his cheeks.  A PIANO BEGINS TO PLAY...
[page_number] 131.

[scene_heading] EXT. WILL’S HOUSE - NIGHT

[action] Fur Elise.  Will recognizes it as he comes up the front walk. 
[action] It comes from INSIDE HIS HOUSE.  He races inside, hopeful, 
[action] past a box of Georgie’s toys left out on the sidewalk.

[transition] CUT TO:

[action] TITLE CARD: 
[centered] SEPTEMBER

[scene_heading] EXT. WILL’S HOUSE - DAY

[action] Zach and Sharon Denbrough pack the last of the camping gear 
[action] on top of the family Wagoneer.

[character] ZACH
[dialogue] Load up!

[action] Will stands at the curb with his gang -- Richie, Ben, Bev, 
[action] Eddie, Stan, and Mike. They hand him a present.

[character] BEVERLY
[dialogue] We all pitched in.

[character] EDDIE
[dialogue] Well Richie mostly.

[character] RICHIE
[dialogue] I had to mow like five hundred 
[dialogue] lawns.  Stan smoked the clippings.

[character (DUAL)] STAN
[dialogue (DUAL)] What does that even mean?
[character (DUAL)] BEN
[dialogue (DUAL)] You gonna open it?

[action] Will does. Inside is a VINTAGE PENTAX CAMERA.

[character] MIKE
[dialogue] We hear Arcadia’s pretty amazing 
[dialogue] this time of year.

[character] BEVERLY
[dialogue] Take some pictures for us.

[character] WILL
[dialogue] Thanks.
[page_number] 132.

[action] They have a group hug.

[character] SHARON
[dialogue] Willy c’mon!

[scene_heading] INT. WILL’S WAGONEER - MOVING - DAY

[action] They pull out of the driveway, camera on Will’s lap. Zach and 
[action] Sharon smile back affectionately from the front seat. 

[action] Will turns to see his friends wave bon voyage with their 
[action] SCARRED HANDS. A reminder of an oath to be kept.

[scene_heading] EXT. WITCHAM & JACKSON STREET/SKY OVER IT - DAY

[action] As Will drives off we RISE UP UP UP over the intersection 
[action] Georgie was killed, HIGH OVER DERRY, until we come to...

[action] A floating RED BALLOON

[action] It POPS!
[page_number] 133.
